target.title: fix(agents): allow empty-error-retry when replayMetadata is absent (#97877)
target.body: Fixes #97877. ## What Problem This Solves `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects` is accumulated across attempts via `observeReplayMetadata`. When a session executes tools on Turn 1 and then hits a transient provider 5xx on Turn 2 (before any tool execution in the current attempt), the cumulative flag is already `true` from Turn 1 — incorrectly blocking the empty-error-retry path even though the failed model call itself never ran tools. ## Why This Change Was Made Four changes to separate current-attempt side effects from cumulative session history: 1. **EmbeddedRunAttemptResult** (types.ts): Added optional `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` — records side effects from the current attempt only. 2. **normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult** (run.ts): Computes `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` from per-attempt raw fields via `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` — the same function `attempt.ts` uses to compute `observedReplayMetadata` before accumulation. The cumulative `replayMetadata` field stays required via `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(raw)` — no contract weakening. 3. **shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn** (incomplete-turn.ts): Prefers `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` to decide whether this specific model call had side effects, falling back to cumulative `replayMetadata` for legacy harnesses. 4. **Runner proof + gateway proof**: Test harness captures `[empty-error-retry]` via `mockedLog.warn`. Live gateway agent with local proxy demonstrates recovery from provider error after prior-turn tool execution. ## User Impact Agents recover from transient 5xx provider errors on any turn — even after prior turns executed tools. The documented `[empty-error-retry]` retry of up to 3 attempts fires when the failed model call itself produced no content, no tool calls, and no delivery side effects. ## Evidence **Behavior addressed**: Cumulative `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects` from prior turns blocks retry of a transient 5xx on a later turn where the model failed before tool execution. **Real environment tested**: Node 22.19+, local OpenClaw checkout. Two proof levels: (1) runner harness with real `runEmbeddedAgent` → `log.warn` capture, (2) live gateway agent with local proxy + real ZTE provider. **Proof 1 — Runner-level: `[empty-error-retry]` log capture** ``` node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 23 passed (23) ``` Key assertions from the real `runEmbeddedAgent` path: ``` ✓ retry on 5xx → mockedLog.warn called with "[empty-error-retry]..." ✓ prior-turn tools + clean 5xx → mockedLog.warn called with "[empty-error-retry]..." (#97877 fix) ✓ current-turn tools → mockedLog.warn NOT called (no duplicate side effects) ``` Only `runEmbeddedAttempt` (HTTP call) is mocked; `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult`, `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`, retry loop, and `log.warn` are all production code. **Proof 2 — Gateway-level: Live agent recovery with real provider** Proxy intercepts chat #3 (Turn 2 first attempt) with plaintext error. Real ZTE Qwen3-235B-A22B API handles all other requests. ``` === Turn 1: run tool → cumulative side effects === [proxy] chat #1 → real API (model decides to run echo hello) [proxy] chat #2 → real API (tool result continuation) hello ← tool output [agent] run ... ended with stopReason=stop === Turn 2: provider error → retry → recovery === [proxy] chat #3 → plaintext body → unclassified → [empty-error-retry] response status=200 contentType=text/plain (not a transport error → runner retry) [proxy] chat #4 → real API (retry after [empty-error-retry]) response status=200 contentType=text/event-stream OK ← recovered reply [agent] run ... ended with stopReason=stop ``` Key observations: - Turn 1 used 2 chats (model call + tool continuation) → side effects accumulated - Turn 2 used 2 chats (plaintext error → retry → real API) instead of 1 - The plaintext body returned HTTP 200 — transport layer does NOT retry HTTP 200 - The runner-level `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` + `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` clean-check allowed retry despite cumulative side effects from Turn 1 - No duplicate tools or side effects — only 4 total chats for 2 turns **Behavior proof (5 scenario matrix)**: ``` [1] currentAttempt clean + cumulative dirty (PRIOR TOOLS) → retry YES ✅ [2] currentAttempt dirty + cumulative dirty (TOOLS RAN) → retry NO ✅ [3] LEGACY (no currentAttempt), cumulative dirty → retry NO ✅ [4] No metadata at all (5xx before tool subsystem) → retry YES ✅ [5] Both clean (clean session) → retry YES ✅ ``` ``` node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 23 passed (23) node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 114 passed (114) node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts ... (all 5 changed files) (passed, exit 0) ``` **Observed result after fix**: The `runEmbeddedAgent` runner produces `[empty-error-retry]` logs when retrying. In the live gateway, a plaintext provider error on Turn 2 (after Turn 1's tool execution) triggers runner-level retry — the agent calls the model again and successfully recovers with `stopReason=stop`. Legacy harnesses without `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` fall back to cumulative `replayMetadata` (backward compatible, no retry when cumulative is dirty). **What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real 5xx HTTP status from the provider. The gateway proof used HTTP 200 + unparseable body (text/plain) to trigger `stopReason="error"` without transport-level retry — this exercises the exact same `unclassified` failover path that a real provider crash would take. ## Regression Test Plan - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run` — 23 passed - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run` — 114 passed - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on all changed files — exit 0 ## Root Cause In `attempt.ts`, `replayMetadataFromState(observeReplayMetadata(getReplayState(), observedReplayMetadata))` merges per-attempt `observedReplayMetadata` with accumulated session-level state before returning. The resulting `rawAttempt.replayMetadata` is always cumulative — `hadPotentialSideEffects` carries the OR of all prior turns. Previously `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` saved `raw.replayMetadata` as `currentAttemptReplayMetadata`, making both fields carry the same cumulative value — the per-attempt check was a no-op. The fix re-derives per-attempt metadata from the raw attempt's observable fields (toolMetas, messaging delivery evidence, session spawns, cron adds) using `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` — the same function `attempt.ts` uses to compute `observedReplayMetadata` before accumulation. ## AI Assistance - **AI-assisted**: Yes - **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) - **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

After gateway restart, sessions with text-only assistant-tail interruptions (agent replied with text, no tool calls, no approval pending) failed silently, forcing users to retype their request. The recovery code only handled raw API message formats and the physical last message, missing text-only canonical content-block representations and trailing system/compaction messages.

This PR recovers from that state by detecting the text-only tail, extracting the last user message from the transcript, and re-presenting it to the agent via `buildResumeMessage()` with an idempotency guard.

Tool-call tails and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed — the broader no-retype recovery policy for side-effecting tails is tracked in #95609.

## Summary

- Problem: Text-only assistant tails after gateway restart forced user retype
- Solution: Extract last user message from transcript and re-present via `buildResumeMessage()` with idempotency guard. Recovery functions now handle both raw API and OpenClaw canonical content-block formats.
- Scope: Text-only recovery. Tool-call and approval-pending tails still fail and prompt retype (#95609 stays open).
- What changed: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts` — `hasAssistantToolCalls()` with 6 canonical block types, `findLastUserMessage()` with content-block array support, `resolveMainSessionResumeBlockReason()` returning meaningful tail, `buildResumeMessage()` idempotency guard + untrusted wrapper, 200-message / 1 MB fallback window, `hasInspectableContent()` guard.

## Evidence

Recovery pipeline proof via production test infrastructure (`pnpm tsx proof-95899-recovery.mjs`):

```console
====================================================================
Gateway Restart Recovery Proof (Text-Only Assistant Tail)
PR #95899 — text-only assistant-tail recovery via re-present
====================================================================

--- Running restart recovery test suite ---

Recovery pipeline tests:

  ✅ recovers from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
  ✅ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has pending tool_calls
  ✅ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has content-block tool_calls
  ✅ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has content-block toolUse
  ✅ recovers from assistant-tail when user message has content-block format
  ✅ recovers from assistant-tail when user message has text-only content blocks
  ✅ gates tool-call recovery when system message trails assistant with tool_calls
  ✅ gates tool_use (snake_case) assistant tails from auto-resume
  ✅ recovers when user message is beyond the recent 20-message window
  ✅ omits idempotency guard when assistant tail has no pending tool_calls
  ✅ falls back to failure when no user message exists in a non-resumable transcript
  ✅ still fails on stale approval-pending tool result tail
  ✅ keeps uninspectable assistant tail fail-closed when content is empty string
  ✅ keeps uninspectable assistant tail fail-closed when content is missing
  ✅ keeps truncated assistant tail fail-closed when content is oversized placeholder
  ✅ keeps truncated assistant tail fail-closed when content block is oversized placeholder
  ✅ keeps truncated assistant tail fail-closed when message has __openclaw.truncated metadata
  ✅ wraps delimiter-shaped recovered content as untrusted prompt data to prevent injection

18/18 recovery tests verified

Tests: 1 passed, 0 failed

--- Recovery behavior matrix ---

| Tail type               | Behavior                    | Proof |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------|-------|
| Text-only assistant     | Re-present user message     | ✅    |
| Tool-call (tool_calls)  | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Tool-call (content)     | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| ToolUse (content)       | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Tool_use (snake_case)   | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Approval-pending        | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Empty/uninspectable     | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Oversized/truncated     | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| No user message         | Fail-closed (resend notice) | ✅    |
| Beyond 20-msg window    | Extended to 200-msg fallback| ✅    |

====================================================================
ALL RECOVERY CHECKS PASSED
====================================================================

Scope: Text-only recovery. Tool-call & approval tails fail-closed.
#95609 stays open for remaining tail types.
```

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro

---

Related to #95609 (text-only recovery — broader no-retype policy stays in #95609)

target.title: fix(whatsapp): Add group admin privacy controls
target.body:
Closes #69233

## What changed

This adds an optional per-group WhatsApp admin override via `channels.whatsapp.groups.<jid>.admin` and wildcard `channels.whatsapp.groups."*".admin`.

When an admin is configured for a group scope:
- the configured E.164 number bypasses mention gating for that group
- non-admin group members cannot run slash commands in that group
- the configured admin remains able to run slash commands for that group

When no admin is configured, the existing group command authorization behavior is preserved.

## Why

The issue described a privacy gap in mixed-trust WhatsApp groups: non-admin members could still probe bot behavior through mentions and slash commands. The first pass of this feature covered the basic config and enforcement, but upstream readiness also needed the behavior pinned down for wildcard group config and for the no-admin-configured fallback path.

## User impact

Users can now designate a WhatsApp group admin by phone number and use that as a privacy boundary for command discovery in group chats.

This is intentionally backwards-compatible:
- groups without `admin` continue using the existing command authorization rules
- exact-group and wildcard group config both work
- docs now describe the new config key

## Root cause

OpenClaw already had group policy, allowlists, and mention gating, but it did not have a per-group owner/admin concept for WhatsApp. That meant there was no narrow way to let a trusted operator manage the bot in a group without exposing command access to other participants covered by broader allowlist rules.

## Validation

- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:core`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:extensions`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- check`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- build`

## AI assistance

- AI-assisted: yes, with Codex
- Testing: fully tested locally with the commands listed above
- Bot review threads addressed and resolved after follow-up fixes

target.title: fix #87407: [Bug]: retry on UND_ERR_SOCKET keep-alive failures
target.body:
**Summary**

- **Problem**: Undici keep-alive pooled sockets can fail with `UND_ERR_SOCKET` on first use after being idle. This causes model requests to fail without retry, cascading through fallback models ([Bug]: Anthropic provider: UND_ERR_SOCKET keep-alive failures trigger silent mid-turn fallback to OpenAI/Codex #87407).
- **Solution**: add `isUndiciSocketError()` detector and one-retry logic in `buildGuardedModelFetch`. On `UND_ERR_SOCKET`, the fetch is retried once with a fresh TCP connection. The local service lease is properly released on retry failure.
- **What changed**: `provider-transport-fetch.ts` — new `isUndiciSocketError()` helper + retry in catch block. `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts` — 8 new tests (5 detection + 3 transport simulation).
- **What did NOT change**: no API, config, schema, or non-undici behavior changes. Only one retry attempt to avoid infinite loops. Non-socket errors propagate immediately without retry.

**Maintainer checklist**

- Fix classification: Root-cause bug fix in provider transport fetch
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High — 172/172 tests pass (2 test files) including 8 new tests

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
Undici keep-alive socket reuse failures (UND_ERR_SOCKET) cause model requests to fail without retry, cascading through fallback models.

**Real environment tested:**
- Platform: Linux x86_64
- Node.js: v22.22.0
- Runtime: Vitest v4.1.8
- Branch: fix/anthropic-undo-socket-87407 (344543b)

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```sh
node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**After-fix evidence:**
```
 ✓ isUndiciSocketError > detects direct UND_ERR_SOCKET code
 ✓ isUndiciSocketError > detects nested UND_ERR_SOCKET in cause
 ✓ isUndiciSocketError > returns false for non-socket errors
 ✓ isUndiciSocketError > returns false for null/undefined
 ✓ isUndiciSocketError > returns false for errors with different codes
 ✓ undici socket retry transport simulation (#97608) > retries once on UND_ERR_SOCKET and succeeds on fresh connection
 ✓ undici socket retry transport simulation (#97608) > throws after UND_ERR_SOCKET retry also fails
 ✓ undici socket retry transport simulation (#97608) > does not retry on non-socket errors

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  172 passed (172)
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- `isUndiciSocketError()` correctly detects direct UND_ERR_SOCKET (code on error) and nested UND_ERR_SOCKET (code on cause) ✓
- `isUndiciSocketError()` returns false for non-socket errors, null/undefined, and different error codes ✓
- **Transport simulation**: first fetch throws UND_ERR_SOCKET → retry once with fresh connection → succeeds (fetchWithSsrFGuard called exactly 2 times) ✓
- **Transport simulation**: both attempts fail with UND_ERR_SOCKET → error propagates (no infinite retry, called exactly 2 times) ✓
- **Transport simulation**: non-UND_ERR_SOCKET errors → no retry (called exactly 1 time) ✓
- All 164 existing tests continue to pass, regression-free ✓

**What was not tested:**
- Live undici connection pool with actual keep-alive timeout (requires integration with a live provider endpoint). The transport simulation covers the full retry code path including lease release and error propagation.

target.title: fix: normalize visible assistant output before delivery
target.body:
## Summary
Gemma was sometimes leaking hidden internal text into Discord replies.
This PR makes OpenClaw clean the final reply in one shared place before sending it.
It adds regression tests for the real bad cases we saw, including leaked `<channel|>` markers and runaway repeated replies.
The fix is shared, not Gemma-only, so every delivery path uses the same cleanup.


## What changed
- teach the shared visible-text normalizer to:
  - strip leaked `<channel|>` control delimiters when they behave like leaked scaffolding
  - preserve literal `<channel|>` mentions in ordinary prose
  - preserve explicitly requested quoted targets, including full repeated strings and visible outputs that end with `<channel|>`
  - honor explicit repeat-count requests like `repeat "ABCD-1234" exactly twice`
  - drop truncated trailing leaked delimiters after long internal preambles
  - keep real leaks visible even when the preamble previously mentioned `<channel|>` literally
  - prefer the minimal repeated unit when a no-delimiter runaway answer repeats a mistakenly doubled exact target many times
  - preserve meaningful leading indentation in delivery mode while still trimming incidental single-space padding
- apply delivery-mode sanitization in the final delivery paths, while keeping stream-update sanitization lighter
- defer chunked `text_end` delivery until the final flush so partial raw scaffold chunks are never emitted early
- mark chunked `message_end` drains as final delivery so the delivery sanitizer runs on the last emitted chunks
- preserve plain streamed chunks without directive parsing when no inline directives are present
- preserve legacy `text_end` reply directives and keep intermediate chunk drains in history mode until final delivery
- reset `blockState` when `text_end` replaces the block buffer with already-sanitized final text, so stale reasoning-tag state cannot strip the final answer on flush

## Regression tests added first
- long prose that literally mentions `<channel|>` stays intact
- `plan: <channel|>visible` is treated as leaked scaffolding
- explicitly requested repeated outputs stay intact when named literally, in quotes, or via repeat-count instructions
- explicitly requested visible outputs that end with `<channel|>` stay intact
- literal marker explanations after an initial leaked delimiter stay intact
- truncated leaked delimiters after long preambles are dropped
- leaked delimiters are still stripped when earlier preamble text mentioned `<channel|>` literally
- repeated exact-target salvage prefers the minimal unit when a no-delimiter runaway answer duplicates the target in the model preamble and then repeats it many times
- legacy `text_end` reply directives still work
- chunked `message_end` drains are marked as final delivery
- intermediate chunk drains keep indentation before `text_end`
- delivery-mode sanitization keeps meaningful leading indentation while still trimming incidental padding
- chunk-boundary reasoning tags do not eat the final flushed answer

## Verification
- failing regressions were added first and reproduced locally before the fixes
- focused tests:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/repeated-visible-suffix.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/repeated-visible-suffix.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.strip-model-special-tokens.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.reply-tags.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.keeps-indented-fenced-blocks-intact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- live Discord probe on `vllm/gemma4-e4b` after restarting the local gateway on this branch:
  - Looper prompt: `reply with exactly pr68986-live2-1776657698 and nothing else`
  - Bob visible Discord reply: `pr68986-live2-1776657698`

target.title: fix(cron): prevent scheduler death when startup catch-up fails
target.body:
## Summary

When runMissedJobs() throws during start(), the timer was never armed, silently killing the cron scheduler until the next gateway restart.

## Root Cause

The start() function in src/cron/service/ops.ts calls runMissedJobs() outside of any try/catch, followed by the locked() block that calls armTimer(state). If runMissedJobs() throws (e.g. an isolated agent turn fails during startup catch-up), execution never reaches armTimer(), and the scheduler is permanently dead.

## Changes

1. **Guard armTimer in start()** - Wrap runMissedJobs() in try/catch so the timer is always armed
2. **Zombie detection in onTimer()** - Clear stale runningAtMs markers at runtime (threshold: 2x job timeout or 2 min) so jobs recover without restart
3. **Tests** - Verify timer stays armed on catch-up failure and stale markers are cleared

Fixes #67854
Related: #59056, #60799

target.title: fix(approval): distinguish policy vs non-persistable reason when Allow Always unavailable
target.body:
Fixes #97069.

## What Problem This Solves

When a command includes shell redirection (e.g. `openclaw --version 2>&1`), Allow Always is correctly hidden because one-shot commands can't be safely persisted. But all nine approval prompt renderers (reply, forwarder, agent-facing, Control UI, reaction, embedded handler, Telegram handler, Telegram forwarding, and gateway API) incorrectly blame the effective approval policy—even when the effective policy is `ask=on-miss`. Users waste time misdiagnosing their approval policy.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is that `resolveExecApprovalAllowedDecisions()` returns the same `["allow-once", "deny"]` for both `ask=always` (policy) and one-shot persistence (non-persistable command). Downstream renderers can't tell the difference.

Rather than introducing a new typed protocol field (`allowAlwaysUnavailableReason`) — which #97081 attempted across 65 files — we thread the existing `ask` field (already present in `ExecApprovalRequestPayload`, `ExecApprovalPendingReplyParams`, `PendingApprovalView`, Control UI request state, and the gateway/node host pipeline) through the approval-pending data pipeline so all renderers can branch on it.

The fix is at the renderer layer, not the policy decision layer. Approval policy logic is unchanged.

## Root Cause

`src/infra/exec-approvals.ts:1824` — `resolveExecApprovalAllowedDecisions()` collapses both reasons (ask=always policy and one-shot persistence) into a single return value. Nine renderers check `!allowedDecisions.includes("allow-always")` and unconditionally emit the policy message.

Introduced by: gumadeiras `ba735d015809` (generic unavailable copy)
Made visible by: jesse-merhi `c9707ab635b9` (one-shot persistence for shell-redirected commands)
Confidence: clear

## User Impact

Users with `ask=on-miss` (or any non-always policy) who run commands with shell redirection will now see:

> Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).

Instead of the misleading:

> The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.

Users with `ask=always` see the same policy message as before.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: Approval prompt text blames policy when missing Allow Always is actually caused by one-shot/non-persistable command safety.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/exec-approval-prompt-text-97069`, base commit `origin/main`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

**Reply renderer** (the function that builds gateway-to-channel approval prompts):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload } from './src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts';

console.log('=== ask=always ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload({
  approvalId: 't1', approvalSlug: 's1',
  ask: 'always', allowedDecisions: ['allow-once', 'deny'],
  command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', host: 'gateway',
}).text);

console.log('=== ask=on-miss ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload({
  approvalId: 't2', approvalSlug: 's2',
  ask: 'on-miss', allowedDecisions: ['allow-once', 'deny'],
  command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', host: 'gateway',
}).text);
"
```

**Forwarder renderer** (real `ExecApprovalRequest` objects through the channel-forwarding path):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { buildExecApprovalRequestMessage } from './src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts';

var now = Date.now();
var req = {
  id: 'exec-req-1', createdAtMs: now, expiresAtMs: now + 300000,
  request: { command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', cwd: '/home/user/project',
    host: 'gateway', agentId: 'main', security: 'allowlist' },
};

console.log('=== ask=always + allow-always excluded ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalRequestMessage(
  { ...req, request: { ...req.request, ask: 'always' } }
));

console.log('=== ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalRequestMessage(
  { ...req, request: { ...req.request, ask: 'on-miss', unavailableDecisions: ['allow-always'] } }
));
"
```

**Evidence after fix** — real production renderer output:

**Reply renderer**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "..."

=== ask=always ===
The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.

=== ask=on-miss (one-shot) ===
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).
```

**Forwarder** (real `ExecApprovalRequest` objects — same shape as the gateway production path):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "..."

=== ask=always + allow-always excluded ===
🔒 Exec approval required
...
Allow Always is unavailable because the effective policy requires approval every time.

=== ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===
🔒 Exec approval required
...
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).
```

**Telegram approval paths** (same renderer called by `buildTelegramExecApprovalPendingPayload` and `approval-handler.runtime.ts`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import ... from './src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts'; ..."

=== Telegram: ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).

=== Telegram: ask=always + allow-always excluded ===
The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.
```

| `ask` input | Message shown |
|---|---|
| `"always"` | "The effective approval policy requires approval every time…" (unchanged) |
| `"on-miss"` (one-shot) | "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted…" (fixed) |
| `"off"` (full-auto with shell redirect) | "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted…" (fixed) |
| absent / null (plugin, legacy) | "The effective approval policy requires approval every time…" (safe default) |

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts \
    src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
    extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
# all passed (with fix in commit 700f47c for approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts — see below)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All nine approval prompt renderers now show the appropriate message. Policy → policy text, one-shot → non-persistable text, absent ask → policy (safe default). Production pipeline (gateway + node hosts → ExecToolDetails → embedded handler) threads `ask` through. The node path uses `approvalDecisionAsk` (`maxAsk(hostAsk, nodeAsk)`) — keeping the reasoning consistent with `allowedDecisions` computation. Telegram native and forwarding paths pass `ask` through `buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload`. Gateway API error path uses the same `isPolicyAlways` heuristic.

Additional CI guard checks pass locally: `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` (dts emit), `pnpm ui:i18n:check` (all locales clean).

**End-to-end Telegram pipeline proof**: The terminal renderer output is byte-identical to what the Telegram Bot API `sendMessage` endpoint receives. The pipeline has three layers with zero text transformations:

1. `buildPendingPayload()` (`approval-handler.runtime.ts:75`) → `buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload({ask, ...})` → returns `{ text, buttons }`
2. `deliverPending()` (`approval-handler.runtime.ts:162`) → `sendMessage(chatId, pendingPayload.text, {...})` — `pendingPayload.text` IS the Layer 1 result, passed verbatim
3. `sendMessageTelegram()` (`send.ts`) → calls Telegram Bot API `sendMessage` — text parameter passed without modification

No escaping, no MarkdownV2 transformation, no string manipulation occurs between layers. Therefore the terminal output above is the exact text Telegram displays.

**Test fix (commit 700f47c)**: The `approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts` test "shows non-persistable reason in reaction prompts" initially used `allowedDecisions: ["allow-once", "deny"]` in the request payload. However, `buildPendingApprovalView()` → `resolveExecApprovalRequestAllowedDecisions()` computes decisions from `ask` + `unavailableDecisions` — it does **not** read `allowedDecisions` from the request. The field was silently ignored, `allow-always` was never excluded, and the test couldn't trigger the non-persistable message path. Fixed by changing to `unavailableDecisions: ["allow-always"]`, which correctly feeds into `resolveExecApprovalRequestAllowedDecisions()`. The other two new tests (reply + forwarder) call `buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload()` directly with `allowedDecisions` in the payload params, not the request — that path uses the field correctly via `resolveAllowedDecisions()`.

## Regression Test Plan

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts \
    src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
    extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
# all pass
```

New test cases added:
- `exec-approval-reply.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason when allow-always is excluded for non-policy reasons"
- `exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason when allow-always excluded for non-policy reasons"
- `approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason in reaction prompts when allow-always excluded for one-shot"

## Changes

- `src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts`: +5/−1 — branch on `params.ask` in reply renderer
- `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts`: +5/−1 — branch on `request.request.ask` in forwarder
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`: +6/−1 — accept `ask` in agent-facing pending message
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts`: +1 — add `ask` to `ExecToolDetails` approval-pending
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts`: +3 — thread `ask` through `buildExecApprovalPendingToolResult`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts`: +1 — pass `hostAsk` to pending tool result
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.ts`: +2 — pass `approvalDecisionAsk` to pending result and request registration (not raw `hostAsk` — matches the `maxAsk(hostAsk, nodeAsk)` value used to compute `allowedDecisions`)
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`: +3 — read and forward `ask` from details
- `src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.ts`: +7/−1 — branch on `ask` in reaction manual instruction, with `approvalKind === "exec"` discriminator guard for `PendingApprovalView` union type
- `extensions/telegram/src/exec-approval-forwarding.ts`: +1 — pass `params.request.request.ask` to reply payload
- `extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts`: +1 — pass `params.view.ask` (exec only) to reply payload
- `src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts`: +10/−7 — distinguish policy vs non-persistable in validateDecision error message
- `ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.ts`: +11/−3 — use `allowAlwaysNonPersistable` i18n key for non-policy
- `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`: +2 — new `allowAlwaysNonPersistable` key
- `ui/src/i18n/`: sync-generated fallback keys for all other locales
- 3 test files: +54 new tests covering one-shot render path

---

**Update (2026-06-30)**: Rebased onto latest main and fixed `ui/src/i18n/.i18n/raw-copy-baseline.json` baseline drift (upstream PR #92654 added "Cancel reply" strings to `chat.ts` but forgot to sync the baseline — not a bug in this PR). All CI checks now pass. No source changes were needed beyond the baseline sync.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): finish timeout-aware setup and status handling
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: after #67815 landed the WhatsApp auth-stability foundation, a few timeout-sensitive WhatsApp surfaces were still not fully coherent.
- Why it matters: setup finalization, logout, heartbeat, configured checks, and status snapshots could still interpret transient auth-stabilizing windows differently.
- What changed: this PR finishes the timeout-aware follow-up for those WhatsApp surfaces, keeps destructive paths safe, and adds focused coverage for the remaining timeout/state reductions.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add QR preflight/result-code behavior or active-QR reuse; those remain in the later PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #67815
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the auth barrier work introduced unstable/timeout outcomes, but a few WhatsApp follow-up decision paths still had inconsistent timeout reductions after #67815.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was not yet one consistent timeout policy across setup finalize, explicit logout, heartbeat auth deps, configured checks, and status/account snapshots.
- Contributing context (if known): #67815 merged a broader base than originally planned, so this PR is now the narrower “finish timeout/state handling” follow-up.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.setup.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.status.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/setup-surface.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/status-issues.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: timeout-backed auth reads do not turn logout into a no-op, setup finalize avoids false QR/relink decisions, heartbeat supports the richer auth-decision seam, and observational status/configured checks stay best-effort.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the remaining regressions live inside the WhatsApp plugin’s timeout interpretation and state reduction logic.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): #67815 covers the broader auth barrier and runtime reconciliation base.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- WhatsApp setup finalization handles auth-stabilizing windows more safely.
- Explicit logout still clears auth state when the barrier times out.
- Heartbeat/configured/status paths stay internally consistent during timeout-backed reads.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[timeout-backed auth read] -> [surface-specific reduction] -> [mixed WhatsApp behavior]

After:
[timeout-backed auth read] -> [surface-specific timeout policy] -> [safe/logout-safe or best-effort configured/status behavior]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp
- Relevant config (redacted): default WhatsApp account config

### Steps

1. Trigger timeout-backed auth reads through WhatsApp setup/status/logout/heartbeat paths.
2. Exercise setup finalize, explicit logout, configured checks, and status snapshots.
3. Verify they use the intended timeout behavior instead of collapsing to a wrong linked/not-linked decision.

target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- `listAllTools` validates each `tools/list` page **once** with `RelaxedListToolsResultSchema` and catalogs **that same validated payload** — there is no strict-then-relist flow, so a stateful or hostile server cannot pass validation with one response and have a different response consumed (no validation-to-use gap).
- `RelaxedToolSchema` is **derived from the pinned SDK's own `ToolSchema`** (`ToolSchema.extend` / `ToolSchema.shape.inputSchema.extend`) and differs from it at exactly one point: the root `inputSchema.type` is an optional string instead of `z.literal("object")`. Every other field — `outputSchema`, task `execution` metadata, `annotations`, pagination — keeps the exact pinned SDK contract, so malformed metadata still rejects the page.
- After every page parse, the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` is invoked with the validated tools — the same side effect `client.listTools()` runs — so `callTool()` enforces output-schema validation and task-required guards identically to the SDK flow. The call is optional (no crash if a future SDK removes the method) and a dedicated test pins the method's existence so a dependency upgrade that drops it fails loudly rather than silently shedding the guards.
- `normalizeInputSchema()` backfills `type: "object"` only when the field is missing; `isValidRootInputSchema()` + the catalog gate skip tools with an explicit non-object root type (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) with a `logWarn`. Server `toolCount` reports accepted tools only.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type` and crashes the whole catalog.

## v6 notes (current revision)
Addresses the P1 from the latest review (validation-to-use gap, confidence 0.96) by adopting its recommended repair: single-response validation that differs from the pinned SDK `ToolSchema` only at the root `inputSchema.type`, with SDK-equivalent metadata caching, explicit non-object filtering, and pagination preserved.
- The strict-then-relist flow is gone entirely; the request count per page is 1 in all cases.
- New stateful-server regression: a server that returns a benign typeless payload on request 1 and a malicious payload on request 2 gets exactly one `tools/list` request, and only the validated payload is cataloged. **Mutation-checked**: against the previous strict-then-relist implementation this test fails with the catalog containing the drifted (never-strictly-validated) tool — reproducing the reported attack — and the server log showing two list requests.
- New SDK-seam pin test: `Client.prototype.cacheToolMetadata` must exist, making the documented private-method dependency an explicit, loudly-failing audit point on SDK upgrades (P1 risk #2 from the same review).
- Relaxation-scope unit tests: malformed `outputSchema`, malformed `execution.taskSupport`, and missing `inputSchema` all still reject the page; pagination (`nextCursor`) keeps the SDK contract.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type` by validating each `tools/list` response once against a schema derived from the pinned SDK contract (relaxed only at the root `inputSchema.type`), backfills `type: "object"`, skips explicit non-object roots, keeps servers with unrelated strict failures rejected, never issues a second list request, and preserves the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects for every page.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from the rebased PR head, Node 25.2.1, pnpm 11.2.2, driving the real bundle MCP runtime (`getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime` → `getCatalog()` → `callTool()`) against four real out-of-process stdio MCP servers, one of them stateful (different payload per request).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on both files, plus a live four-server e2e script via `pnpm exec tsx`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  42 passed (42)
pnpm tsgo:core / tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint / oxfmt --check: clean

e2e (four live stdio servers):
tools: ["legacySdkServer/search_docs","driftServer/benign_typeless_tool"]
servers: {"legacySdkServer":{"toolCount":1},"driftServer":{"toolCount":1}}
rejected: ["badOutputServer","mixedServer"]
driftServer tools/list requests received: 1        // exactly one — no second-request window
callTool(search_docs) → McpError -32600: Tool search_docs has an output schema but did not
  return structured content                        // metadata cache populated from the same response
```

Observed result after fix: A `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` catalogs cleanly with `type: "object"` backfilled; an explicit `type: "array"` sibling is skipped with a logWarn; servers with malformed `outputSchema` (alone or mixed with a repairable tool) stay rejected; a stateful server attempting response drift receives exactly one list request and only its validated payload is cataloged; `callTool()` enforces `outputSchema` for typeless tools exactly as the SDK strict flow would.

What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.

target.title: feat(plugins): add session followup turn API and gateway-restart extension
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()` method to the plugin runtime, enabling plugins to schedule proactive agent turns for any session — including cold sessions with no active user interaction.

> **Supersedes #60951** — rebased onto latest main, all CI issues resolved, bug fixes included.

## Problem

Plugins have no way to inject a user-role message into an existing session and trigger an agent turn. The available primitives (`enqueueSystemEvent`, `subagent.run`) either don't initiate turns or create new sessions instead of continuing the original conversation. This blocks use cases like:

- **Post-restart session resume** — an agent restarts its own gateway and needs to automatically continue working after the restart completes
- **Model backend switching** — an agent swaps inference backends (e.g., switching between local LLMs on a single-GPU workstation) and needs to resume the session on the new model with full context
- **Proactive plugin notifications** — a service detects an external event and needs to wake a sleeping session
- **Deferred task completion** — a plugin schedules follow-up work after a long-running background operation

## Solution

### Core API: `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()`

A new `followup` namespace on `PluginRuntimeCore` that:

1. **Reconstructs a full `FollowupRun`** from the session store via `buildFollowupRunForSession()` — resolving agent identity, workspace, provider/model, session file, and delivery context from the persisted `SessionEntry`.

2. **Enqueues the run** into the existing followup queue infrastructure with preserved queue mode (respects `mode` of any existing queue for the session key, defaults to `"followup"`).

3. **Handles cold-start drain** by detecting whether the session's followup queue is already draining. If not (cold session), creates a fresh followup runner with preloaded session metadata and a no-op typing controller, then kicks the drain loop.

```typescript
// Plugin usage
const enqueued = await api.runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn({
  sessionKey: "agent:my-agent:telegram:direct:12345",
  prompt: "Background task complete. Here are the results...",
  source: "my-plugin",
});
```

### Bundled Extension: `gateway-restart`

A reference implementation that demonstrates the followup turn API:

- **Tool** (`gateway_restart`): Agent calls this to run optional allowlisted pre-commands, write a restart marker (only after commands succeed), then exit the process. Systemd (or equivalent) auto-restarts the gateway.

- **Service** (`gateway-restart-watcher`): On startup, checks for the restart marker. If found, calls `enqueueFollowupTurn()` to resume the original session with a completion message. Marker is deleted only after successful enqueue — preserved for retry on failure.

Disabled by default (`enabledByDefault: false`). Opt-in via `plugins.entries.gateway-restart.enabled: true`.

## Files Changed

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.ts` | New: cold-session FollowupRun builder with session store resolution |
| `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.ts` | New: plugin runtime followup namespace with hot/cold session detection |
| `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` | Add `followup` type to `PluginRuntimeCore` |
| `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` | Wire `createRuntimeFollowup()` |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/index.ts` | New: gateway-restart plugin (tool + watcher service) |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/openclaw.plugin.json` | New: plugin manifest |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/package.json` | New: package metadata |
| `test/helpers/plugins/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` | Add `followup` to mock |
| `**/**.test.ts` | 24 unit tests across 3 test files |

## Bug Fixes (vs #60951)

1. **Marker written after pre-commands** — prevents stale markers when a pre-command fails
2. **Marker deleted after enqueue confirmed** — preserves marker for retry if `enqueueFollowupTurn()` returns false
3. **Hot session guard** — `rememberFollowupDrainCallback` only registers for cold sessions; existing hot-session drain runners are not overwritten
4. **Queue mode preservation** — `enqueueFollowupRun` respects existing queue mode instead of forcing `"followup"`
5. **Session metadata preload** — cold followup runners receive preloaded session store and entry for accounting/refresh paths

## Testing

- 24 unit tests across 3 test files (all passing)
- `pnpm check` passes (tsgo + oxlint + all architecture checks)
- Rebased onto latest main
- End-to-end verified on a local workstation running OpenClaw 2026.4.8 with Gemma 4 26B via llama.cpp

## Use Cases This Enables

This API is the foundation for several plugins currently in development:

- **`model-switch`** — seamless switching between local LLMs on single-GPU workstations, with session continuation via `enqueueFollowupTurn()` after the new model is healthy
- **`inference-guard`** — priority-aware request scheduling for single-slot inference (heartbeat/cron defer behind user messages)
- **Self-modifying agents** — agents that update their own config, restart the gateway, and validate changes automatically

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run extensions/gateway-restart/index.test.ts` — 6 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] Gateway-restart tool writes marker only after pre-commands succeed
- [ ] Watcher service enqueues followup turn and deletes marker on success
- [ ] Watcher preserves marker on enqueue failure

## AI Disclosure

This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude Code). Testing level: **fully tested** — 24 unit tests, `pnpm check` green, end-to-end verified on local hardware. The author understands all code in this PR and can explain design decisions.

target.title: fix: honor filePath/path/media fallbacks in outbound reply normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- `normalizeOutboundReplyPayload` only checked `payload.mediaUrl`, silently dropping media when agents returned `filePath`, `path`, or `media` fields
- This caused WhatsApp (and other channel) auto-replies to send plain text instead of attachments (`hasMedia: false` in outbound logs)
- Added the same fallback chain already used by the message-send action runtime (`media` → `mediaUrl` → `filePath` → `path`)

## Root cause

The action runtime in `channel-DuaAdRTE.js` correctly maps all four parameter names:
```ts
const mediaUrl = readStringParam(params, "media") 
  ?? readStringParam(params, "mediaUrl")
  ?? readStringParam(params, "filePath")
  ?? readStringParam(params, "path");
```

But `normalizeOutboundReplyPayload` in `reply-payload.ts` only checked `mediaUrl`:
```ts
const mediaUrl = typeof payload.mediaUrl === "string" ? payload.mediaUrl : undefined;
```

This meant that when an agent's conversational reply included media via `filePath` or `path` (common when using tool results), the auto-reply delivery path silently dropped the attachment.

## Test plan

- [x] Added 6 unit tests covering all fallback paths and precedence
- [x] All 30 tests in `reply-payload.test.ts` pass
- [ ] Verify WhatsApp attachment sends work with `filePath` parameter on a live gateway

target.title: fix(gateway): use already-aborted signal in stopChannel fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- In `stopChannel`, when no `AbortController` exists for an account (i.e. no running task), the fallback `new AbortController().signal` creates a signal from an anonymous controller that **can never be aborted**
- `stopAccount` implementations may use this signal for cleanup coordination (checking `signal.aborted`, passing to `fetch`, using with `sleepWithAbort`, etc.)
- A never-aborting signal can cause `stopAccount` to hang or timeout during graceful shutdown instead of completing immediately

### Fix

Replace `new AbortController().signal` with `AbortSignal.abort()`, which returns an already-aborted signal. This correctly communicates "this account is being stopped" to the plugin's `stopAccount` implementation.

### Context

On line 485, `abort?.abort()` is called — so if `abort` exists, its signal is already aborted. The fallback only fires when `abort` is undefined (no active task for this account). In both cases the intent is the same: tell `stopAccount` to shut down. `AbortSignal.abort()` makes the fallback consistent with the primary path.

## Test plan

- [x] All 14 tests in `server-channels.test.ts` pass
- [x] One-line change, minimal risk
- [x] `AbortSignal.abort()` is available since Node 15.12.0; project requires Node 22+

[AI-assisted]

Adds opencode-go DeepSeek V4 thinking-profile hooks so `xhigh` and `max` are accepted for DeepSeek V4 Flash/Pro.

PR surface: Source +46. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main's opencode-go provider lacks a thinking profile, the generic fallback cannot add `max`, and explicit `/think max` is rejected when the provider/model profile lacks that level.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A mechanical repair can refresh the branch, preserve current main's wrapper chain, and add targeted provider-profile coverage without a product or security decision.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): add supportedReasoningEfforts to DeepSeek V4 model entries
target.body:
## Problem

DeepSeek V4 Flash and V4 Pro natively support reasoning_effort: "max" at the API level. OpenClaw's stream layer already maps xhigh/max to "max" on the wire via resolveDeepSeekV4ReasoningEffort(). However, the opencode-go provider did not expose these levels through the thinking validation layer, causing /think xhigh and /think max to be rejected.

## Solution

Follow the same pattern used by the OpenRouter extension: add supportsXHighThinking and resolveThinkingProfile hooks to the opencode-go provider registration, declaring a thinking profile that includes xhigh and max for DeepSeek V4 models.

## Changes

- extensions/opencode-go/thinking-policy.ts (new) — Thinking level definitions for DeepSeek V4 models (off through max).
- extensions/opencode-go/index.ts — Added supportsXHighThinking and resolveThinkingProfile hooks.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

Thinking level "max" was previously rejected for opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash with the error "Thinking level "max" is not supported". After the fix, setting thinkingDefault: "max" is accepted and the session activates with Think: max without errors.

### environment

- OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (eeef486)
- Provider: opencode-go
- Model: deepseek-v4-flash
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64)
- Running as systemd user service on a KVM VPS

### steps

1. Applied the equivalent patch to the compiled extension JS at dist/extensions/opencode-go/index.js, adding supportsXHighThinking and resolveThinkingProfile hooks matching the source change in this PR.
2. Set thinkingDefault: "max" in openclaw.json.
3. Restarted the OpenClaw gateway.
4. Verified session status shows Think: max active.
5. Verified no "not supported" errors from the thinking validation layer.

### evidence

Session status output:

```
Model: opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash
Think: max
```

Config:

```
"thinkingDefault": "max"
```

### observedResult

Think: max is accepted and active. The session applies thinking level "max" and the stream wrapper sends reasoning_effort: "max" on the wire for all API requests.

### notTested

Stream-level reasoning_effort payload was verified via code inspection of resolveDeepSeekV4ReasoningEffort() which maps both xhigh and max to the string "max". Actual wire capture was not performed.

Related: #76482

target.title: doctor(memory): surface FTS5 unavailable state in doctor checks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #62328 by making `openclaw doctor` visibly report when SQLite FTS5 is unavailable in the running gateway.

- Extend `doctor.memory.status` payload with `fts` probe fields (`enabled`, `available`, `error`)
- Thread that payload through `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` in doctor command flow
- Add a dedicated doctor warning in `noteMemorySearchHealth` when gateway reports FTS unavailable
- Include targeted guidance for `no such module: fts5` / missing `ENABLE_FTS5`

## Why

Without this, users can see memory embeddings as ready while FTS keyword/BM25 fallback is silently unavailable. This change surfaces the condition and gives concrete next steps.

## Tests

- Added/updated test in `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` to assert `fts` is returned in `doctor.memory.status`
- Added test in `src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts` to assert doctor emits an FTS5 warning when gateway probe reports unavailable
- Local targeted verification:
  - `corepack pnpm exec vitest src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts -t "returns gateway embedding probe status for the default agent"`
  - `corepack pnpm exec vitest src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts -t "warns when gateway probe reports FTS unavailable"`

## Notes

One unrelated existing test in `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` is currently path-sensitive on this Windows environment (`falls back to the manager workspace...`) and fails independently of this change. The new/affected targeted cases pass.

target.title: feat(gateway): allow chat.inject to override provider attribution via originAgent
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an optional `originAgent` field to `ChatInjectParamsSchema` and persists it as a **separate, dedicated attribution field** on the injected assistant transcript entry. The pre-existing `(provider: "openclaw", model: "gateway-injected")` sentinel pair is **preserved unchanged**, so the runtime replay filter at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts:271` continues to correctly drop these rows from being replayed back to the next model call.

UI avatar/handle resolvers can key off the new persisted `originAgent` field to render the true source of a cross-agent inject (e.g. Hermes, Codex) instead of always showing the OpenClaw avatar.

## Why

`chat.inject` is the canonical RPC for cross-agent transcript writes. Today the persisted entry has `provider: "openclaw"` hard-coded, with `additionalProperties: false` on the schema closing the door on any caller-supplied attribution. When external agents (Hermes, Codex, Claude Code, etc.) inject via this RPC, the message renders with OpenClaw's avatar and the only visible attribution is whatever the caller embeds in body text. This breaks the "who said what" guarantee in long transcripts.

Discovered in production by an external agent (Hermes) attempting a cross-agent verify ping into a webchat session. The body-tag workaround ("FROM: Hermes —") works at-a-glance but degrades attribution clarity over time and across many entries.

## Behavior

- **With `originAgent` set:** persisted entry gets a new `originAgent: "<value>"` field. `provider` and `model` are unchanged.
- **Without `originAgent`:** behavior is byte-identical to current main. No new field is written. Fully backward compatible.
- **Empty / whitespace-only input:** schema rejects literal `""` (`minLength: 1`); persistence helper additionally trims whitespace and treats blanks as absent, so a malformed value never lands on disk even if a future schema change loosens validation.

## Why a separate field, not provider override

The Codex review on the prior commit caught a real correctness defect: `provider` is part of the runtime replay contract, not just display. `normalizeAssistantReplayContent` (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts:271`) drops gateway-injected rows from model replay only when `provider === "openclaw"` and the model is one of `{ "delivery-mirror", "gateway-injected" }`. Overwriting `provider` would have caused transcript-only injects to leak into model context, duplicating content and risking provider ordering rejections.

The redesigned patch keeps the sentinel pair untouched and adds attribution as a dedicated parallel field. UI consumers read the new field; the replay filter is unaffected.

## Security

`chat.inject` is already gated at operator-privilege scope (`src/gateway/method-scopes.ts`). Anyone who can call it can already write arbitrary assistant content into a session transcript. `originAgent` is **purely presentational** and introduces no new impersonation surface beyond what current authorized callers already possess. Because the sentinel pair is preserved, the previously-flagged security risk (UI-only injects entering model replay) does not apply to this revision.

## Files

- `src/gateway/protocol/schema/logs-chat.ts` — schema field with `minLength: 1`, `maxLength: 100`
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` — handler param + helper threading
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts` — new persisted field, sentinel preserved, trim+empty normalization
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift` — regenerated via `pnpm protocol:gen:swift`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cross-agent `chat.inject` calls render with the OpenClaw avatar in the operator's webchat UI because the persisted assistant entry has no caller-controlled attribution field. External agents currently have no way to mark the inject as their own without putting "FROM: Hermes —" inline in body text, which degrades transcript clarity. This patch adds a dedicated `originAgent` persisted field while preserving the `(provider: "openclaw", model: "gateway-injected")` sentinel that the replay-history filter relies on.

**Real environment tested:** Sandboxed checkout of openclaw main at `/tmp/openclaw-pr/openclaw-fresh` with the patched files and the regenerated protocol bindings. Used the actual openclaw runtime — `appendInjectedAssistantMessageToTranscript` and `validateChatInjectParams` are invoked via `node` through `tsx` against synthetic transcript fixtures created with the project's own `createTranscriptFixtureSync` helper. No mocks, no stubs, no real session keys, no production data.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/proof-chat-inject-origin-agent.mts
node node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/proof-chat-inject-schema.mts
```

Both scripts are committed under `scripts/` in this PR so a maintainer can rerun them.

**Evidence after fix:** Live stdout captured from the runtime invocations above. Persistence-layer output shows the actual `provider`, `model`, and `originAgent` fields read back from the jsonl transcript file on disk after the patched code wrote to it. Schema-layer output is the AJV validator's verdict on real input shapes.

Persistence-layer terminal output (live runtime, the patched code path actually executed):

```
OpenClaw chat.inject originAgent attribution — real-behavior proof
Patched modules under test:
  src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts
  src/gateway/protocol/schema/logs-chat.ts (schema field)
  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts (handler threading)

Design:
  provider stays 'openclaw' (replay-filter sentinel preserved)
  originAgent is a NEW persisted field for display-only attribution
  empty/whitespace originAgent is normalized to absent

=================================================
CASE                  : baseline-no-origin
input.originAgent     : <unset>
append.ok             : true
messageId             : 09641925-2611-4401-86c6-d369a045b518
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : <absent>
expected.originAgent  : absent
result                : PASS

=================================================
CASE                  : hermes
input.originAgent     : "hermes"
append.ok             : true
messageId             : fcd260be-a790-489b-82e5-f393d136e321
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : hermes
expected.originAgent  : hermes
result                : PASS

=================================================
CASE                  : codex
input.originAgent     : "codex"
append.ok             : true
messageId             : ef02a786-0e71-48d3-aafb-b861049d8301
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : codex
expected.originAgent  : codex
result                : PASS

=================================================
CASE                  : blank-empty-string
input.originAgent     : ""
append.ok             : true
messageId             : 9943b96b-9bfc-4050-a997-b362c10e2422
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : <absent>
expected.originAgent  : absent
result                : PASS

=================================================
CASE                  : whitespace-only
input.originAgent     : "   "
append.ok             : true
messageId             : 2aa2899d-a2f7-4679-a404-4236009f8c79
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : <absent>
expected.originAgent  : absent
result                : PASS

=================================================
CASE                  : trim-surrounding-whitespace
input.originAgent     : "  hermes  "
append.ok             : true
messageId             : 4b1f86b7-060d-4cce-a81a-0cfcfb70ddca
stamped.provider      : openclaw
stamped.model         : gateway-injected
stamped.originAgent   : hermes
expected.originAgent  : hermes
result                : PASS
```

Schema-layer terminal output (live AJV validator on patched schema):

```
ChatInjectParamsSchema validator behavior:

---
CASE     : legacy params (sessionKey + message + label)
accepted : true
---
CASE     : patched params (with non-empty originAgent)
accepted : true
---
CASE     : rejects empty-string originAgent (minLength:1)
accepted : false
errors   : [{"instancePath":"/originAgent","schemaPath":"#/properties/originAgent/minLength","keyword":"minLength","params":{"limit":1},"message":"must NOT have fewer than 1 characters"}]
---
CASE     : rejects unknown extra fields (additionalProperties:false preserved)
accepted : false
errors   : [{"instancePath":"","schemaPath":"#/additionalProperties","keyword":"additionalProperties","params":{"additionalProperty":"somethingMadeUp"},"message":"must NOT have additional properties"}]
```

**Observed result after fix:**

- Baseline call (no `originAgent`) → persisted entry has `provider: "openclaw"`, `model: "gateway-injected"`, no `originAgent` field. Byte-identical to current-main behavior; replay filter still matches.
- Patched call (`originAgent: "hermes"`) → persisted entry has `provider: "openclaw"`, `model: "gateway-injected"`, **and** a new `originAgent: "hermes"` field. Replay filter still matches the sentinel pair and drops the row; UI consumers can attribute it to Hermes via the new field.
- Patched call (`originAgent: "codex"`) → same as above with `originAgent: "codex"`. Confirms the field is not Hermes-specific.
- Blank input (`""` or whitespace-only) → persisted entry has no `originAgent` field. The persistence helper trims and normalizes, so malformed input cannot land on disk.
- Trimmed input (`"  hermes  "`) → persisted entry has `originAgent: "hermes"` (whitespace-stripped).
- Schema validator accepts non-empty `originAgent`, rejects literal `""` via the new `minLength: 1`, and continues to reject unknown extra fields via the unchanged `additionalProperties: false`.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end UI render in the actual webchat surface (visually confirming a Hermes avatar/handle in the live React UI keyed off the new `originAgent` field) is intentionally out of scope for this PR — the patch only adds the persisted attribution field; UI consumption is a follow-up that will pick up the field from the transcript. The replay-filter regression that the prior PR revision would have introduced is now structurally impossible because the sentinel pair is unchanged; no run-time replay test was added because the contract being preserved already has coverage in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.test.ts`.
