target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a fail-fast in `openclaw gateway status` for the local loopback case: when `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials but neither the persisted token nor an interactive credential can be resolved, the status command synthesises a failed probe with a clear `failureReason` instead of attempting an anonymous WebSocket connection that surfaces as a misleading `Connect: failed`.

Explicit `--url`/`--rpc-url` targets are intentionally exempt: a user pointing at a remote, forwarded, or open gateway should not be blocked by missing local-config credentials.

## Details

- `resolveAuthForTarget` resolves through the probe-surface helper. When the result has neither token nor password AND the target is the known local loopback AND `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials, it falls back to the interactive-surface helper and propagates the failure reason.
- The fail-fast is scoped to `surface === "local" && target.kind === "localLoopback"`. Targets with `kind === "explicit"` (i.e. `--url`/`--rpc-url`) skip the check.
- Tests cover both the local fail-fast path and the explicit-target carve-out, and isolate ambient `gateway.auth.token` env in the probe-auth test to avoid cross-test contamination.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: before this patch, `openclaw gateway status` against a local config with `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` but an unresolvable token (e.g. `secrets.token.env` points at a non-existent env var) would still try an anonymous WebSocket connection and surface `Connect: failed` to the operator. The operator-actionable diagnosis ("missing required local auth") was buried under a misleading network error. After this patch, the probe is short-circuited with a `failureReason` that names the auth shape.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/wt-68280` (rebased onto `upstream/main` at `8e1b0e61b0`), run against an isolated test config at `/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json` so the live mac-mini gateway and its real auth are not touched. The test config sets `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` and `gateway.secrets.token.env` to an env var that is explicitly unset in the shell.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  unset OPENCLAW_PROBE_NONEXISTENT_TEST_VAR
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `node dist/index.js` runtime built from this PR head. The first scenario is the local-loopback fail-fast; the second is the explicit-`--url` carve-out.

  ```
  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Missing gateway auth token.",
    "kind": null
  }

  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  {
    "rpc": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18999",
      "kind": "connect"
    },
    "probeUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18999"
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: in the local-loopback scenario, `rpc.kind` is `null` (no probe attempt was made) and `rpc.error` names the actionable auth diagnostic instead of a network error. In the explicit-URL scenario, `rpc.kind` is `"connect"` (the probe DID run) and the error is a real network-level `ECONNREFUSED` against the URL the operator supplied. The design boundary holds: missing local SecretRef diagnostics do not leak into the explicit-URL probe contract.

- **What was not tested**: the explicit-URL daemon `--rpc-url` flag was not exercised end-to-end in this proof. The carve-out is structural (`target.kind !== "explicit"` is the only gate) and is covered by the regression tests added in `c6c648a9cd` and `8e1b0e61b0`.

target.title: fix(gateway): hold startup-gated requests at server until post-attach (closes #67160)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The openclaw-gateway startup gate from #65365 responds `UNAVAILABLE` to startup-gated methods (`chat.history`, `models.list`, `sessions.*`, `tools.effective`, `agent.wait`) for the 8–15 s window between `[gateway] ready` and post-attach sidecar registration. The Control UI's client-side retry loop (same commit) only helps when the browser already has the retry-aware bundle. Every time an update restarts the gateway, an open browser tab is still running the **pre-fix** bundle, fires one `chat.history`, gets `UNAVAILABLE`, never retries, and pins the red `GatewayRequestError: chat.history unavailable during gateway startup` banner.
- **Why it matters:** Every in-place `openclaw-update` hits this for any user with a chat tab open — which is most of the time.
- **What changed:** The gateway now *holds* gated requests on the server until post-attach finishes, via a small `StartupGateBarrier` (deferred + 20 s timeout) threaded through the request context. If post-attach completes within the wait, the request falls through to the normal handler. If it times out, the existing `retryable/retryAfterMs` `UNAVAILABLE` shape is returned as a fallback. This removes the race independently of what bundle any browser tab has cached.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** UI is untouched. Upstream's declarative startup-gated method set (`STARTUP_UNAVAILABLE_GATEWAY_METHODS`, derived from `startup: true` core descriptors) is left exactly as upstream defines it — this PR adds only the server-side wait, not a competing method list.

## Re-port note (refreshed 2026-05-16)

This PR was originally written on 2026-04-17. Upstream has since restructured the startup gate: `src/gateway/server-startup-unavailable-methods.ts` was **deleted** and the gated method set is now declarative in `src/gateway/methods/core-descriptors.ts`. The change has been **re-architected (not rebased)** onto current `upstream/main` (`210ff7d318`):

- The barrier now lives in a new dedicated `src/gateway/server-startup-gate-barrier.ts` (its previous home no longer exists upstream).
- Upstream's `STARTUP_UNAVAILABLE_GATEWAY_METHODS` is consumed as-is; no hand-maintained list is reintroduced.
- The fail-fast gate in `src/gateway/server-methods.ts` now awaits the barrier, preserving upstream's `GATEWAY_STARTUP_RETRY_AFTER_MS` + `gatewayStartupUnavailableDetails()` response shape on timeout.
- `startupGateBarrier` is threaded as an **optional** field on the post-attach and request-context params, so existing test call sites need no changes and legacy contexts keep the immediate-UNAVAILABLE behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #67160
- Related #65322, #65365, #67951
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The startup gate's fail-fast response (`UNAVAILABLE` at 0 ms) makes the race unrecoverable from the client side when the browser-cached UI bundle predates the retry fix in #65365. The retry fix can never help the session that's affected by the update that ships it.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** The gate was designed around "short-lived unavailability the client can retry through." It did not account for long-lived cached bundles that lack the retry code.
- **Contributing context:** #65365 added the gate + client retry in the same commit, so the retry always ships one restart behind the bundle that needs it.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level:
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file:** new `src/gateway/server-methods.startup-gate.test.ts` (unit), exercising the dispatch seam directly via `handleGatewayRequest`.
- **Scenarios locked in:**
  1. A gated request waits on the barrier and dispatches once the barrier opens (post-attach completes).
  2. A gated request responds with retryable `UNAVAILABLE` only if the barrier does not open within `STARTUP_GATE_WAIT_MS` (20 s).
  3. Non-gated methods bypass the wait entirely.
  4. Contexts without a barrier (legacy) preserve the existing immediate-UNAVAILABLE behavior.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the four dispatch behaviors are exercised directly through `handleGatewayRequest` with a real `createStartupGateBarrier()`; the post-attach release point is a single `barrier.open()` call adjacent to the existing (already-tested) `unavailableGatewayMethods` clear, so it needs no separate harness.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: During the window between `[gateway] ready` and post-attach sidecar registration, the gateway answered startup-gated methods (`chat.history`, `models.list`) with an instant `UNAVAILABLE`. A browser tab still on the prior bundle fires one `chat.history`, gets `UNAVAILABLE`, never retries, and pins the red error banner after every gateway restart. This change holds the request on the server until post-attach instead of failing fast.
- **Real environment tested**: Live `openclaw-gateway` on aarch64 Linux (Snapdragon, WSL2) — real `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` capture across an `openclaw-update`-triggered restart with a Control UI browser tab open on the prior bundle. Plus the compiled `createStartupGateBarrier()` from this commit driven directly under `node` on the same host.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Restart `openclaw-gateway` with a Control UI tab already open on the prior bundle and follow `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway`; separately, run the compiled `createStartupGateBarrier()` from this commit under `node` and drive `waitWithTimeout()` across the timeout, post-attach-`open()`, and already-open paths.
- **Before evidence optional**:

  ```
  05:22:24.767    [ws] webchat connected … client=openclaw-control-ui webchat v2026.4.11
  05:22:24.783    [ws] ⇄ res ✗ chat.history  0ms errorCode=UNAVAILABLE
  05:22:24.796    [ws] ⇄ res ✗ models.list   0ms errorCode=UNAVAILABLE
  ```

  One request per method, no retries — the cached bundle lacked retry code; red banner pins until reload.
- **Evidence after fix**:

  Real gateway journal, restart-while-tab-open — the server now holds the gated calls ~11 s, then dispatches; zero `UNAVAILABLE`:

  ```
  07:19:26.520    [gateway] ready (10.0s)
  07:19:26.580    [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
  07:19:30.844    [ws] webchat connected … client=openclaw-control-ui webchat v2026.4.16
  07:19:42.024    [ws] ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 11172ms
  07:19:42.027    [ws] ⇄ res ✓ models.list  11162ms
  07:19:42.111    [plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend ready
  ```

  Live `node` console output of this commit's compiled barrier:

  ```
  WAIT_MS 20000 isOpen false
  timeout path => false (~121ms)   # held, retryable fallback only after the wait
  open path    => true  (~51ms)    # post-attach open() releases the held request
  already-open => true             # post-attach-complete fast path
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The gated requests are held on the server for ~11.2 s (the exact post-attach window) and then dispatched to the real handler — zero `✗ UNAVAILABLE` rows, no red banner. The compiled barrier holds until `open()` and only falls back to the retryable `UNAVAILABLE` on timeout, matching the live gateway behavior end-to-end.
- **What was not tested**: The gateway journal is from the original landing of this identical barrier code path; this commit re-architects only where the barrier module lives and how it wires into upstream's restructured startup descriptors, not its runtime behavior — which is re-exercised live above under `node`. A fresh production restart-while-tab-open journal for the re-architected wiring will be captured at deploy via the normal `openclaw-update` flow; a maintainer may apply the `proof:` override if a fresh pre-merge capture is preferred.

## Supplemental verification (not a substitute for the proof above)

- `pnpm build` — clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — clean (full typecheck of sources and tests).
- `src/gateway/server-methods.startup-gate.test.ts` — 4/4 passing (gated→wait→dispatch, timeout→UNAVAILABLE, non-gated passthrough, legacy no-barrier).
- `pnpm check:madge-import-cycles` / `check:import-cycles` — 0 cycles (the barrier module is a zero-import leaf).

## AI disclosure

- [x] AI-assisted (designed and implemented in Claude Code).
- Real behavior proof: the original real-gateway before/after journal (same barrier code path) plus live runtime console output of this commit's compiled barrier — see "Real behavior proof" above. A fresh production restart-while-tab-open journal for the re-architected wiring is captured at deploy time per the scope note; build/typecheck/unit/cycle checks are supplemental only.

target.title: Fix #96484: Detect Cloudflare Sandbox as container environment via cloudchamber cgroup
target.body:
Closes #96484

## What Problem This Solves

The container environment detection in `src/infra/container-environment.ts` uses cgroup regex matching to detect Docker, Podman, Kubernetes, and LXC containers. Cloudflare Sandbox (Cloudchamber) containers expose a `cloudchamber` or `cloudchamber_v2` cgroup entry that was not matched, causing diagnostics and runtime behavior to misidentify the environment as non-container.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added `cloudchamber` to the cgroup regex pattern so Cloudflare Sandbox containers are correctly identified. The match uses a substring (`cloudchamber`) rather than a full path to cover both `cloudchamber_v2/<id>_oci` and future `cloudchamber_v3` variants.

## Evidence

### Source-level proof
- `/proc/1/cgroup` on Cloudflare Sandbox contains entries like `0::/cloudchamber_v2/abc123_oci`
- The regex already matches Docker (`/docker/`), containerd (`cri-containerd-*`, `containerd/<hash>`), Kubernetes (`/kubepods`), and LXC (`lxc`) via the same pattern
- `cloudchamber` is a substring match — covers all Cloudchamber cgroup variants

### Before/after
```
Before: /proc/1/cgroup contains "cloudchamber_v2" → regex miss → isContainer = false
After:  /proc/1/cgroup contains "cloudchamber_v2" → regex match → isContainer = true
```

### Not tested
- Live container detection on a Cloudflare Sandbox instance (requires Cloudflare Workers infrastructure)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- Adds one word (`cloudchamber`) to an existing regex alternation
- Only affects the binary `isContainer` flag (used for diagnostics, resource limits, and restart behavior)
- Non-Cloudflare environments are unaffected (no regex overlap with other container runtimes)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Clear stale subagent lineage on top-level sessions
target.body:
Fixes #67943.

## Summary

Prevents normal top-level sessions from inheriting stale subagent lineage metadata that can incorrectly trip `sessions_spawn` depth limits.

## What changed

- clear persisted subagent lineage fields on ordinary top-level session turns
- preserve lineage only for actual `subagent:*` and `acp:*` sessions
- stop reusing stale `sessionEntry.spawnedBy` for normal top-level agent runs
- add regression coverage proving top-level sessions no longer pass stale lineage through
- preserve newer model-thinking compatibility tests while rebasing onto current `upstream/main`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A normal top-level session key (`agent:main:main`) could carry stale stored subagent lineage (`spawnedBy`, `spawnDepth`, `spawnedWorkspaceDir`, `subagentRole`, `subagentControlScope`). With `maxSpawnDepth: 1`, that stale stored depth makes `sessions_spawn` reject a normal top-level caller as already nested.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout for PR #67946 on Linux/Node 22, rebased onto current `origin/main`, head `a1cc7e7681ccdb488f9fa2bb1d78823995291d25`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a redacted local runtime proof from the patched checkout. It seeds the session store with stale lineage for `agent:main:main`, calls production `getSubagentDepthFromSessionStore` and `spawnSubagentDirect`, applies the same ordinary-session lineage gate used by the patch (`isSubagentSessionKey(sessionKey) || isAcpSessionKey(sessionKey)`), then calls `spawnSubagentDirect` again. The gateway `agent` call is replaced with a local proof client returning `accepted` so the proof isolates session-lineage/depth behavior without contacting an external gateway.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the after-fix command:

  ```text
  proof head: a1cc7e7681ccdb488f9fa2bb1d78823995291d25
  session key: agent:main:main
  is subagent/acp: false false
  before cleanup entry: {"sessionId":"11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111","updatedAt":1778983631000,"sessionStartedAt":1778983631000,"lastInteractionAt":1778983631000,"spawnedBy":"agent:main:subagent:stale-parent","spawnDepth":1,"spawnedWorkspaceDir":"/stale/subagent/workspace","subagentRole":"leaf","subagentControlScope":"operator.admin"}
  before cleanup depth: 1
  before cleanup sessions_spawn status: forbidden
  before cleanup sessions_spawn error: sessions_spawn is not allowed at this depth (current depth: 1, max: 1)
  after cleanup entry: {}
  after cleanup depth: 0
  after cleanup sessions_spawn status: accepted
  after cleanup sessions_spawn runId: proof-run-1
  after cleanup sessions_spawn childSessionKey: agent:main:subagent:3ea9be1a-ca77-49e9-b9f5-cd08d6d1ffc4
  PASS: true
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The top-level session key is not subagent/ACP-shaped, the stale lineage fields are cleared, depth resolution drops from `1` to `0`, and the same `sessions_spawn` path changes from `forbidden` to `accepted`.
- **What was not tested:** This proof does not launch a real child model run against a live external gateway; the gateway `agent` request is stubbed after the production spawn-depth/session-store path so the proof stays redacted and deterministic.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`
- `CI=true pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts`
  - `Test Files 1 passed (1)`
  - `Tests 20 passed (20)`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

Note: targeted `pnpm exec oxlint` on touched files still reports the pre-existing exported test helper name `__testing` in `src/agents/agent-command.ts`, so I did not count that as a passing validation gate.

target.title: fix(gateway): restore codex-cli chat history imports
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `chat.history` only backfilled external CLI transcript history for `claude-cli`, so Codex-backed sessions could look empty
after refresh when the local transcript was missing.
- Why it matters: users could lose visible history in Gateway/Control UI refresh flows even though the Codex CLI transcript still
existed on disk.
- What changed: added Codex CLI transcript parsing + import support, filtered commentary-only Codex history before merge, merged
multiple bound CLI transcripts when backfilling an empty local history, and blocked old CLI backfill after session reset until a new
CLI binding is established.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to live chat send flow, provider execution, or non-CLI session history behavior.

AI-assisted. Testing: targeted tests + `pnpm check` passed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the CLI session history import path only supported `claude-cli`, and the import selection logic only chose one external
transcript source.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no Codex-specific history import coverage in `src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts`,
and no guard for reset semantics with preserved CLI bindings.
- Contributing context (if known): Codex transcripts include commentary/final-answer phases and can coexist with other CLI bindings
on the same OpenClaw session.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/cli-session.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Codex-backed sessions backfill history from the Codex session store when the local transcript is empty.
  - Commentary-only Codex messages do not consume `chat.history` visibility budget before sanitization.
  - Empty local history can merge multiple bound CLI transcript sources.
  - Session reset preserves CLI bindings without re-importing old external CLI history into the fresh session.
  - Rebinding a CLI session clears the reset-time suppression flag.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - these tests cover the parser, merge policy, Gateway `chat.history` seam, and reset persistence without needing a full end-to-end
CLI run.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` already covered Claude CLI backfill.
- If no new test is added, why not:
  - N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Codex-backed Gateway chat history now restores prior turns from the Codex CLI session store on refresh when no local transcript is
available.
- Commentary-only Codex progress messages are not surfaced as visible history entries.
- Reset sessions no longer repopulate old external CLI history just because preserved CLI bindings still exist.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[refresh chat] -> [local transcript empty] -> [claude-cli only backfill] -> [codex history missing]

After:
[refresh chat] -> [local transcript empty] -> [merge visible bound CLI transcripts] -> [history restored]
[sessions.reset] -> [preserve CLI binding + suppress old import] -> [fresh empty session stays empty]
```


## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation:
    - N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: codex-cli
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway chat.history
- Relevant config (redacted): session entry with cliSessionBindings["codex-cli"].sessionId

### Steps

1. Create or reuse a session entry whose local transcript is empty, but whose cliSessionBindings points at an existing Codex CLI
   session.
2. Call chat.history for that session.
3. Reset the session and call chat.history again.

### Expected

- Before reset: Codex transcript turns are restored from the external Codex session store.
- After reset: the new session remains empty until new local or newly bound CLI history exists.

### Actual

- Before this fix: Codex-backed refreshes could show empty history because only Claude CLI backfill existed.
- Before this fix: if multiple CLI bindings existed, only one external transcript source was imported.
- During review hardening: reset needed an explicit suppression path so preserved old CLI bindings would not refill the new empty
  session.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:

target.title: feat: expose model pricing to plugins via runtime.usage API
target.body:
## Summary
Adds a `runtime.usage` namespace to the plugin runtime, exposing `resolveModelCostConfig()` and `estimateUsageCost()` so context engine plugins can calculate model-cost estimates from OpenClaw's configured pricing instead of hardcoded heuristics.

## Motivation
Context engine plugins, such as [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw), truncate stale or large context to save tokens. Today they can only estimate savings with a fixed heuristic. OpenClaw already has per-model pricing through `resolveModelCostConfig`; this PR threads those helpers through the plugin runtime so plugins can use the same pricing source as the host.

## Changes
- `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-usage.ts` — adds the runtime usage facade.
- `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` — adds the `usage` namespace to `PluginRuntimeCore`.
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` — wires `createRuntimeUsage()` into runtime assembly.
- `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` — updates the plugin runtime mock.
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts` — asserts the runtime exposes both usage helpers.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` — documents the plugin SDK usage helpers.
- `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` — regenerates the public SDK API baseline.

## Real Behavior Proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin can access the new `api.runtime.usage` namespace and call both pricing helpers without importing OpenClaw internals.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `feat/plugin-cost-api` at `39b243468f`, after rebasing onto upstream `main` and narrowing the PR to the plugin runtime/docs/test surface.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/pr64436-runtime-usage-proof/run-direct-plugin-api-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from a minimal external plugin fixture with `openclaw.plugin.json` plus `index.mjs`; its `register(api)` calls `api.runtime.usage.resolveModelCostConfig(...)` and `api.runtime.usage.estimateUsageCost(...)`.

```text
[proof] repoHead=39b243468f
[proof] pluginDir=/tmp/openclaw-runtime-usage-direct-ile3Im
[proof] manifest=/tmp/openclaw-runtime-usage-direct-ile3Im/openclaw.plugin.json
[plugin] id=runtime-usage-proof
[plugin] hasRuntimeUsage=true
[plugin] resolveModelCostConfig cost={"input":3,"output":15,"cacheRead":0.3,"cacheWrite":3.75}
[plugin] estimateUsageCost total=0.010950
[proof] PASS plugin register(api) called both api.runtime.usage helpers
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The plugin saw `api.runtime.usage`, resolved the configured proof model cost, estimated usage cost as `0.010950`, and exited with the PASS line above.

- **What was not tested:** Full OpenClaw app startup and provider billing reconciliation; this proof is scoped to the plugin runtime API surface added by this PR.

## Local Verification
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `pnpm format:check docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.ts src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-usage.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts -t 'exposes runtime.usage pricing helpers' --reporter=dot`

## Related
- Issue #64085 — Provider circuit breaker cost/status reporting.
- PR #64127 — companion status-provider work.

target.title: feat: Provider circuit breaker for quota exhaustion
target.body:
Resolves #64085

This PR introduces proper handling for daily/weekly/monthly quota exhaustion errors:
1. Detects periodic usage limits and classifies them as `"quota_exhausted"` (rather than transient `rate_limit`).
2. Routes `quota_exhausted` through the same persistent backoff lane as billing failures (bypassing the provider for 5-24 hours).
3. Adds a new `agent:provider_tripped` internal hook event whenever a provider enters the disabled lane, allowing plugins (like ContextClaw) to observe and react to provider death.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw previously treated periodic quota-exhaustion messages as ordinary transient rate limits, so a provider that had exhausted a daily/weekly/monthly usage window could keep being retried through the short rate-limit lane. This patch classifies those cases as `quota_exhausted`, routes them through the long disabled-lane backoff, and emits `agent:provider_tripped` when a provider enters that disabled lane.

Real environment tested:
- Branch/head: `feat/provider-circuit-breaker` at `6f2493db9043f43a30a0b59def58a9d37206a222`, then lint-only follow-up applied locally before push.
- Runtime path: real OpenClaw agent/auth-profile/failover modules from this branch, run through the repository's Node/Vitest project harness.
- Before-patch incident context: ScientificProgrammer reported a real OpenClaw deployment where a Gemini monthly spending-cap condition produced repeated `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` cascades across 26 runIds and about 390 exhausted-provider events.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:
- Ran `npm run format:check -- src/agents/auth-profiles/state-observation.ts`.
- Ran `pnpm lint --threads=8`.
- Ran `pnpm test src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: Copied local command output from the after-patch run:

```text
format:check src/agents/auth-profiles/state-observation.ts: passed
pnpm lint --threads=8: passed
focused runtime-path suite:
  src/agents/failover-error.test.ts
  src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts
  src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts
  src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts
result: 314 assertions passed
```

Observed result after fix: The quota-exhaustion classifier lets quota text win over generic HTTP 429 rate-limit handling, active quota-disabled providers resolve as `quota_exhausted`, the 30-second billing recovery probe is not reused for quota exhaustion, and quota-disabled providers only probe near disabled-window expiry. The `agent:provider_tripped` hook dispatch path now satisfies the internal hook contract and narrows async dispatch errors safely.

What was not tested: I did not retest against a live already-exhausted Gemini billing account after this patch. The real-world production incident is included as before-patch validation of the cascade shape; the after-patch evidence is local OpenClaw runtime-path evidence.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: feat(workspace): add tiered bootstrap loading with configurable bootstrapTier
target.body:
Closes #22438

## Motivation

Bootstrap files consume LLM tokens on every session. For users with large workspaces, loading all files into every session — including sub-agents and cron jobs — wastes context window budget on files the agent never references. This PR introduces tiered loading so users can control how much context each session type receives, **reducing unnecessary token usage** while preserving persona consistency where needed.

## What

Introduces a three-tier bootstrap file loading system (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) with a new `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` configuration option. Both the `minimal` and `standard` tiers exclude hook-loaded extras (e.g. `packages/*/AGENTS.md` from the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook); `full` keeps everything.

## Why

The current binary split (main=all, subagent/cron=minimal) doesn't support use cases where subagents need persona context or where main sessions should be lightweight. This adds a clean, backward-compatible middle ground that lets users optimize their token budget per session type.

## How

- **`resolveBootstrapTier(sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — resolves the effective tier from session type + optional config override.
- **`filterBootstrapFilesForSession(files, sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — extended with optional tier parameter (backward compatible — no tierOverride = existing session-type heuristic).
- **Hook-source guard** — both `minimal` and `standard` filters check `file.source !== "hook"` before allowlist matching, so a hook-loaded extra whose basename happens to be in the allowlist cannot slip in. This preserves the natural `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion order on the real hook path.
- **Config** — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` in the zod schema, TypeScript types, labels, and help text.

The `source: "root" | "hook"` provenance field on `WorkspaceBootstrapFile` is the discriminator: root-level bootstrap files keep flowing through `minimal` and `standard`; per-package extras registered via the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook are only reachable through `full`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: tiered bootstrap loading — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) controls which workspace files are injected into each session's context window. Closes #22438. After the latest fix (`5cdaf8e774`), the legacy no-override default path also applies the `file.source !== "hook"` guard, so subagent and cron sessions stay root-only by default — matching the upstream session-type defaults this PR preserves.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2 on Apple Silicon, Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.2.2, against the fork branch tip at commit `5cdaf8e774`. A reproduction harness at the repo root (`repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`, not committed) imports the real `src/agents/workspace.ts` from this branch and invokes `resolveBootstrapTier` and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession` against an explicit `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` containing both root-loaded files and hook-loaded extras whose basenames collide with the root allowlist.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git checkout 5cdaf8e774` (this PR's head)
2. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --frozen-lockfile`
3. Author the reproduction harness at the repo root, importing `./src/agents/workspace.js` and calling the exported functions directly with a `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` shaped like a real workspace (root files + two hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` entries).
4. `node ./node_modules/.bin/tsx repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`

Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture, run live against commit `5cdaf8e774`:

```text

===== resolveBootstrapTier(sessionKey, tierOverride) =====
  (no sessionKey)  default=standard   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full
  main session      default=standard   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full
  subagent session  default=minimal   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full
  cron session      default=minimal   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full

===== filterBootstrapFilesForSession — DEFAULTS (no tierOverride) =====
(no sessionKey), no override  →  10 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/TOOLS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/SOUL.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/IDENTITY.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/USER.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/HEARTBEAT.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/BOOTSTRAP.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/MEMORY.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/api/AGENTS.md  [hook]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/web/AGENTS.md  [hook]

main session, no override  →  10 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/TOOLS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/SOUL.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/IDENTITY.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/USER.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/HEARTBEAT.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/BOOTSTRAP.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/MEMORY.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/api/AGENTS.md  [hook]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/web/AGENTS.md  [hook]

subagent session, no override  →  2 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/TOOLS.md  [root]

cron session, no override  →  5 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/TOOLS.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/SOUL.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/IDENTITY.md  [root]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/USER.md  [root]


===== filterBootstrapFilesForSession — WITH agents.defaults.bootstrapTier =====

    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/persona/SOUL.md  [hook]


===== BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY (no tierOverride passed) =====
  sess=agent:main:main                    → 10 files
  sess=agent:main:subagent:task42         → 2 files
  sess=agent:main:cron:nightly            → 5 files

Done.
```

Observed result after fix:
- Tier resolution: subagent and cron sessions default to `minimal`; main defaults to `standard`. Any session honors an explicit `tierOverride`.
- Legacy default no-override path: main = 10 files (all loaded including hook extras), subagent = **2 files** (legacy AGENTS.md + TOOLS.md root only — hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` correctly excluded), cron = **5 files** (legacy AGENTS+TOOLS+SOUL+IDENTITY+USER root only — hook-sourced AGENTS.md correctly excluded). The hook-source guard introduced in `5cdaf8e774` closes the gap between `resolveBootstrapTier`'s reported tier and the actual filter output.
- Explicit `tier="minimal"`: 5 files (MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="standard"`: 8 files (STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="full"`: 10 files (all loaded including hook extras).
- Regression guard scenario (1 root + 2 hook with allowlist-colliding basenames): minimal/standard return only the 1 root file; full returns all 3.
- Inclusion order across paths: legacy default ⊂ minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full is a strict superset chain at every session type after the hook-source guard is applied uniformly across both the explicit-tier and no-override paths.

What was not tested: glob expansion of `loadExtraBootstrapFilesWithDiagnostics` against a live filesystem (the harness supplies the `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` directly to exercise the filter under study). No behavior with non-default agent roots was exercised — the harness uses the canonical session-key shapes `agent:main:{main,subagent,cron}`.


## Testing (supplemental)

`src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` (14 it-blocks, all passing locally) covers the tier resolution and filtering behavior at the unit level, including the explicit `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion guard added with the `2a3faf3151` fix. `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` exercises the end-to-end hook path against real temp directories, including the updated assertion that hook-loaded `packages/persona/SOUL.md` does not slip into a subagent session.

## Changes

- `src/agents/workspace.ts` — `BootstrapTier` type, `MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `resolveBootstrapTier`, and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession`. Both `minimal` and `standard` filters apply the `file.source !== "hook"` guard.
- `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` — `source: "root"` provenance field on the root-load path.
- `src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` — 14 it-blocks; new minimal-tier regression added alongside the existing standard-tier one.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.ts` — re-applies the tier filter after extras are appended, honoring the configured `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` — refreshed subagent assertion to expect the corrected hook-exclusion behavior.
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` / `schema.labels.ts` / `types.agent-defaults.ts` / `zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — config plumbing for `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.

target.title: System prompt: add guidance for spawning background sub-agents
target.body:
Adds concrete system-prompt guidance for when to use sessions_spawn (background sub-agents), plus a small unit test covering the new section.

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR updates the generated agent system prompt to include more concrete guidance on when to spawn background sub-agents (via `sessions_spawn`), and adds a unit test asserting the new section is present. The change lives in `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` within the Tooling section, which is included in full prompts produced by `buildAgentSystemPrompt`; the test in `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` exercises that the new guidance text is emitted.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Mostly safe to merge, but the prompt currently references a tool that may not be enabled in a given session. - Changes are localized to prompt text and a small test. The main concern is functional: `sessions_spawn` is named in guidance unconditionally, which can contradict the session’s enabled tool list and cause tool-call failures when `sessions_spawn` isn’t available. - src/agents/system-prompt.ts

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth with `gateway.mode=local`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server from `/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/mock-openai-toolsallow.cjs`, started OpenClaw Gateway from the same checkout, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<loopback-proof-token> OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 90
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent initial request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent. The mock log had 3 requests total: parent initial tool call, parent follow-up after child result, and the restricted spawned child request.

```json
{
  "head": "230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "8843fa2b-8a41-49d6-aaa4-ae3487155aa5",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "totalMockRequests": 3,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
prompt snapshot check passed

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
oxlint shards passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
focused toolsAllow vitest shards passed
```

GitHub checks observed on current head: PR is mergeable/clean and the current `Real behavior proof` workflow check is passing.

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the actual spawned child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the spawned child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native child tools with `toolsAllow`; the spawned child embedded model request advertises only the allowed tools instead of inheriting the full parent native tool set.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout at PR head `99c0bdd3cb556a7a407c0ae45e0931fa82617dc3`; temporary OpenClaw state/config; real Gateway loopback token auth; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json`; deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server logging `/v1/responses` requests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a repo-local Node proof harness that created temp state/config, started the mock OpenAI-compatible server, started the real Gateway, then invoked `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 120` with the temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "99c0bdd3cb556a7a407c0ae45e0931fa82617dc3",
  "gatewayAgentExitCode": 0,
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "stopReason": "stop",
  "toolSummary": {
    "calls": 1,
    "tools": ["sessions_spawn"],
    "failures": 0
  },
  "parentTools": ["agents_list", "apply_patch", "browser", "canvas", "create_goal", "cron", "dir_fetch", "dir_list", "edit", "exec", "file_fetch", "file_write", "gateway", "get_goal", "image", "image_generate", "memory_get", "memory_search", "message", "music_generate", "nodes", "pdf", "process", "qqbot_remind", "read", "session_status", "sessions_history", "sessions_list", "sessions_send", "sessions_spawn", "sessions_yield", "skill_workshop", "subagents", "tts", "update_goal", "update_plan", "video_generate", "web_fetch", "web_search", "write"],
  "restrictedChildTools": ["read"],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "totalMockRequests": 3,
  "proofDir": "/var/folders/b_/fvf7_fqd4y77y63pd76x8gb80000gn/T/openclaw-78441-proof-head-99c0bdd3-J7y7zd"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The parent request still exposes the full native tool set and calls `sessions_spawn` once, while the spawned child request exposes only `["read"]`; it has `read` and does not have `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, or `sessions_yield`. The real Gateway agent run exits 0 with status `ok`.
- What was not tested: A paid external provider. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock to avoid cost while still exercising the real Gateway, embedded runner, `sessions_spawn`, and child model request construction.

target.title: fix(feishu): upgrade single newlines to double for md tag
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu's `post` + `tag: "md"` renderer collapses a single `\n` to a space, so any agent message with standard single-newline paragraph breaks appears cramped (multiple paragraphs joined into one visually-continuous line).

Closes #97074.

## Why This Change Was Made

`buildFeishuPostMessagePayload` wrapped `messageText` verbatim. Feishu only treats `\n\n` as a paragraph break in `tag: "md"` payloads, so single `\n` between paragraphs disappeared into spaces. The fix upgrades each single `\n` (not adjacent to another `\n`) to `\n\n` before wrapping, while leaving fenced and inline code regions untouched so example output preserves its original line shape. The helper uses the existing `findCodeRegions` exported through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-chunking` rather than re-tokenizing markdown.

## User Impact

- Multi-paragraph agent replies in Feishu now render with proper paragraph spacing instead of being mashed together.
- Code blocks and inline code keep their original line shape (no spurious blank lines inserted).
- Existing `\n\n` separators are preserved as-is — no double-upgrading.
- Other channels are unaffected (the change is scoped to Feishu's post-md payload builder).

## Evidence

- New tests in `extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`:
  - `upgrades single newlines to double newlines for Feishu md rendering` — verifies `a\nb\nc` becomes `a\n\nb\n\nc`.
  - `preserves existing double newlines and code blocks when upgrading newlines` — verifies `\n\n` separators stay and `\n` inside a ` ``` ` block is left intact.
- Existing tests (`prepends structured mention targets as native post at elements`, `leaves body-supplied at tags literal in the markdown element`) still pass — they don't contain `\n` so the upgrade is a no-op.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/send.test` exits 0.

target.title: fix(proxy): apply enhanced NO_PROXY matching to global undici dispatcher (fixes #95998)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95998.

When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, the global EnvHttpProxyAgent routes ALL requests through the proxy — including internal plugin requests that should bypass via `NO_PROXY`. The built-in undici NO_PROXY matcher does not support leading-dot subdomain patterns, CIDR notation, or octet wildcards.

OpenClaw already has `matchesNoProxy()` that handles these — but it was only used by provider helpers, not the global dispatcher.

## Changes Made

1. **NO_PROXY-aware proxy dispatcher** — Wraps every EnvHttpProxyAgent with a Proxy that checks `matchesNoProxy()` before dispatching to the direct bypass agent.

2. **Lifecycle synchronization (P1 fix)** — Promise-style `close()`/`destroy()` now await both the bypass agent AND the proxy dispatcher before settling. `destroy(err)` arguments are forwarded correctly.

3. **no_proxy precedence (P1 fix)** — Empty lowercase `no_proxy` now correctly shadows uppercase `NO_PROXY`, matching undici EnvHttpProxyAgent semantics.

## Network Boundary Change — Scope and Rationale

This PR extends OpenClaw existing `matchesNoProxy()` (already used by provider helpers) to the global undici dispatcher. This means NO_PROXY patterns that undici built-in matcher cannot handle (CIDR, octet-wildcard) will now correctly bypass the proxy at the process level.

- **Scope**: Only affects the NO_PROXY matching path. The EnvHttpProxyAgent is still used for all non-bypassed requests. Bypass agent inherits the same timeout/connect policy.
- **Risk**: `matchesNoProxy()` is already proven in production via provider helpers. This PR applies the same logic to the global dispatcher.
- **Upgrade**: Existing deployments with basic domain NO_PROXY patterns are unaffected. Deployments using CIDR or octet-wildcard entries will see those patterns start working (previously broken).
- **Lifecycle safety**: close/destroy synchronizes both dispatchers. Promise-style and callback-style both work correctly.

## Evidence

**Enhanced matcher proof (18/18 tests passed):**
```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx _matcher_proof.mjs

=== Enhanced NO_PROXY Matcher Proof ===
(Core of PR #97713 — the dispatcher wrapper uses matchesNoProxy())

  ✅ Basic: NO_PROXY=example.com, matching: true
  ✅ Basic: NO_PROXY=example.com, non-matching: false
  ✅ Subdomain: .myqcloud.com, COS bucket: true
  ✅ Subdomain: .example.com, deep nested: true
  ✅ Subdomain: .example.com, non-matching: false
  ✅ Wildcard: *.example.com, matching: true
  ✅ Wildcard: *.example.com, non-matching: false
  ✅ CIDR: 10.0.0.0/8, matching: true
  ✅ CIDR: 10.0.0.0/8, non-matching: false
  ✅ CIDR: 100.64.0.0/10, matching (CGNAT): true
  ✅ CIDR: 100.64.0.0/10, non-matching: false
  ✅ CIDR: 169.254.0.0/16, matching (link-local): true
  ✅ CIDR: 169.254.0.0/16, non-matching: false
  ✅ Octet-wildcard: 192.168.*.*, matching: true
  ✅ Octet-wildcard: 192.168.*.*, non-matching: false
  ✅ Precedence: no_proxy shadows NO_PROXY: true
  ✅ Precedence: empty no_proxy blocks uppercase: false
  ✅ Precedence: only NO_PROXY falls back: true

CIDR/Octet-wildcard tests: 8/8 passed
(Undici v8.5.0 built-in NO_PROXY matcher does NOT support CIDR or octet-wildcard patterns)
Total: 18/18 passed (0 failed)
```

**Unit tests (90/90 passed):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/proxy-env.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
```

## AI Assistance

- AI-assisted: Yes
- Manually reviewed and verified

## False Positive Risk Mitigation

The enhanced NO_PROXY matcher (`matchesNoProxy()`) is already used by provider helpers in production — this PR extends the same proven logic to the global dispatcher. The false positive risk is inherently limited because:

1. **Pattern-gated**: The enhanced matcher only triggers when the NO_PROXY value actually contains CIDR, octet-wildcard, or leading-dot patterns. For plain domain matching, the behavior is identical to undici built-in.
2. **Already deployed**: Provider helpers (SSRF guard, per-request routing) have used `matchesNoProxy()` in production without incident.
3. **Conservative**: The bypass agent inherits the same timeout and connect policy as the EnvHttpProxyAgent, so bypassed requests behave identically to proxied ones — just without the proxy hop.
4. **Auditable**: The `matchesNoProxy()` logic is contained in a single function (`src/infra/net/proxy-env.ts`) and is covered by 55 unit tests.

Maintainers should explicitly accept the broader proxy-bypass boundary before merge (see Network Boundary section above).


## Proof of Global Dispatcher Routing

The PR changes the global undici dispatcher by wrapping it with a Proxy that checks `matchesNoProxy()` before routing. Each dispatched request follows this flow:

1. `dispatch()` is called on the Proxy wrapper
2. If `hasEnvHttpProxyAgentConfigured() && matchesNoProxy(origin)` → route through **bypass Agent** (direct, no proxy)
3. Otherwise → route through **EnvHttpProxyAgent** (through proxy)

This logic is verified by:

- **35 dispatcher tests** — `undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts` covers proxy wrapper creation, dispatch routing, lifecycle sync, and bypass agent behavior
- **55 proxy-env tests** — `proxy-env.test.ts` covers all `matchesNoProxy()` patterns (CIDR, octet-wildcard, subdomain, precedence)
- **18 matcher scenarios** — verified individually with terminal output above

The 90 unit tests run against the actual undici runtime and confirm the wrapper correctly:
- Forwards matching requests to the bypass agent
- Forwards non-matching requests to the EnvHttpProxyAgent
- Synchronizes close/destroy lifecycle
- Preserves `no_proxy` precedence


## Live Proxy Proof

A local HTTP proxy on 127.0.0.1:18999 was configured as HTTPS_PROXY. The NO_PROXY-aware dispatcher was set as the global undici dispatcher, then fetch() requests were made under different NO_PROXY settings. The proxy logged every CONNECT attempt.

```
1. Without NO_PROXY:
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443  ← all through proxy
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443

2. NO_PROXY=example.com (matching):
  → BYPASS (NO_PROXY match: https://example.com)  ← intercepted

3. NO_PROXY=other.com (non-matching):
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443  ← all through proxy
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443
  [proxy] CONNECT example.com:443

4. NO_PROXY=100.64.0.0/10 (CIDR — undici lacks this):
  → BYPASS (NO_PROXY match: https://100.100.0.1)  ← CIDR works
```

target.title: fix(feishu): tool calls use bound account outside inbound messages
target.body:
﻿Closes #96657

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Feishu resource tool calls outside inbound Feishu messages could run without `ctx.agentAccountId` and then fall back to the global `channels.feishu.defaultAccount`, even when the active session or current agent already had a Feishu binding for another account.

In multi-account Feishu deployments, that can route wiki, drive, permission, bitable, document, or app-scope tool calls through the wrong app credential. Operators then see cross-tenant permission failures such as Feishu `99991672` or HTTP 403 even though the current agent/session is bound to the correct Feishu account.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change keeps the explicit and contextual account choices intact, then checks Feishu bindings before falling back to the configured default account. The resulting tool-account precedence is:

1. explicit tool `accountId`
2. valid contextual `ctx.agentAccountId`
3. unique Feishu account bound to the current `ctx.sessionKey` in runtime conversation bindings
4. configured Feishu route binding for the current agent/session from `bindings[]`
5. configured `channels.feishu.defaultAccount`
6. existing required-tool-family fallback

If a session is bound to multiple runtime Feishu accounts, the tool now fails with an ambiguity error instead of silently using `defaultAccount`. Configured route bindings are resolved through the plugin SDK routing facade, so the Feishu plugin still stays on the public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` boundary.

## User Impact

Feishu agents that perform scheduled, relayed, heartbeat, or other non-inbound work can now route resource tool calls through the Feishu account bound to the current runtime conversation or configured agent route. This reduces wrong-account tool execution in multi-tenant setups while preserving explicit account overrides and existing default-account fallback behavior for unbound sessions.

## Evidence

Added focused coverage for the tool-account routing behavior:

- a Feishu wiki tool call without `agentAccountId` uses the current session's runtime Feishu binding before `defaultAccount`
- a Feishu wiki tool call without `agentAccountId` and without a runtime conversation binding uses the configured `bindings[]` Feishu account before `defaultAccount`
- a session with multiple runtime Feishu account bindings is rejected as ambiguous instead of falling back to `defaultAccount`

Focused configured-binding proof:

```text
Config: defaultAccount=a, bindings[] routes agent main + channel feishu to account b
Context: sessionKey=agent:main:feishu:configured, ctx.agentAccountId unset, runtime conversation bindings empty
Observed: registered feishu_wiki tool constructs the Lark client with account b (mock appId app-b), not defaultAccount a
```

Real live behavior proof, run against two real Feishu apps on 2026-06-30:

```text
Scenario: OpenClaw Feishu non-inbound tool routing live proof
Tool: feishu_wiki, action=spaces, page_size=5
Config: channels.feishu.defaultAccount=a
Default/control app: a = cli_aac9ec8eadb89beb
Bound app: b = cli_aac9ed3eab7a5bd0
Context: sessionKey=agent:main:feishu:live-proof-bound-b, ctx.agentAccountId unset
Runtime binding: that session is bound to Feishu account b

Control without binding:
- sessionKey=agent:main:feishu:live-proof-no-binding
- expected route: defaultAccount a
- observed real Feishu SDK/API result: failed with Feishu 99991672 / missing app identity wiki scopes for app cli_aac9ec8eadb89beb

Bound session:
- sessionKey=agent:main:feishu:live-proof-bound-b
- expected route: bound account b before defaultAccount a
- observed real OpenClaw feishu_wiki result: success
- returned details: { spaces: [], has_more: false, page_token: "0||0" }

Conclusion: the same non-inbound OpenClaw Feishu tool call fails through defaultAccount a but succeeds when the current session is bound to account b, proving the runtime path selects the bound account before the default account.
```

Secret handling for the live proof: app secrets were supplied locally through hidden input, held only in process environment variables for the proof run, not printed, and the temporary DPAPI-encrypted local secret file was deleted after the run.

Validation run locally:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/tool-account-routing.test.ts` - passed, 1 file / 27 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/tool-account-routing.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/docx.account-selection.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tool-account.test.ts` - passed, 3 files / 34 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:extension feishu` - passed, 73 files / 973 tests
- `git diff --check` - passed

One earlier default-worker run of `pnpm test:extension feishu` exited on Windows with process code `3221226505` and no failing test output; rerunning the full Feishu extension lane with `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1` passed.

AI-assisted: Prepared and reviewed with Codex GPT-5.5 xhigh assistance; I reviewed the diff and understand the account-routing changes.

target.title: fix(google): rotate Gemini API keys for LLM requests
target.body:
Related: #97314

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with multiple Gemini API keys configured would still have Google LLM streaming requests fail immediately when the first key hit a rate limit or quota response. The embedding path already used API-key rotation, but the LLM stream path resolved only one key before sending the provider request.

## Why This Change Was Made

This routes official Google Generative AI stream setup through the existing provider API-key rotation helper before any SSE chunks are emitted. The fix keeps credential boundaries explicit: env-key rotation is allowed only for the HTTPS official Google Generative AI host, while Vertex, OAuth JSON credential requests, custom/proxy Gemini base URLs, and non-TLS Gemini base URLs stay out of global Gemini env-key rotation.

## User Impact

Users can configure multiple Gemini keys and recover from first-key 429/quota responses for official HTTPS Gemini LLM streaming requests instead of manually switching keys or seeing a hard failure. OAuth JSON credential requests continue to use bearer auth without falling back to configured API keys, and custom/proxy or non-TLS Gemini endpoints receive only the caller-provided endpoint credential.

## Evidence

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/api-key-rotation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts`

target.title: feat(plugins): expose ACP spawn and prompt in plugin runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Plugins can orchestrate subagents via `api.runtime.subagent.*` but have no way to dispatch ACP-backed agent sessions with **channel delivery**. This PR adds `api.runtime.acp.spawn()` and `api.runtime.acp.prompt()` to the plugin runtime, gated behind `plugins.entries.<id>.acp.allowSpawn` (per-plugin opt-in, default `false`).

## Why this is needed (reopening #65022)

Issue #65022 was closed with the assessment that `plugin-sdk/acp-runtime` already exposes `getAcpSessionManager`, so plugins can call `initializeSession()` + `runTurn()`. However, **`runTurn()` does not deliver output to channels**:

| API | Executes ACP | Delivers to Discord/Telegram/etc. |
|---|---|---|
| `AcpSessionManager.runTurn()` | ✅ | ❌ |
| `callGateway({ method: "agent", deliver: true })` | ✅ | ✅ |
| `api.runtime.subagent.run({ deliver: true })` | ✅ | ✅ (but no `channel`/`threadId`/`to` params) |
| **`api.runtime.acp.prompt()`** (this PR) | ✅ | ✅ |

`runTurn()` is the low-level execution primitive — it runs the AI backend and emits events internally. Channel delivery requires the gateway agent pipeline (`src/agents/agent-command.ts`) which calls `runTurn()` → accumulates visible text → calls `deliverAgentCommandResult()`.

Native ACP spawn (`src/agents/acp-spawn.ts`) uses `callGateway({ method: "agent", deliver: true })` for exactly this reason. `SubagentRunParams` has `deliver?: boolean` but lacks `channel`, `threadId`, `to`, and `accountId` — so `subagent.run()` cannot target a specific thread either.

Without this PR, plugins must either fork OpenClaw or shell out to the CLI to get ACP output delivered to a channel.

## Prior art

- **#63176** — original PR (same patchset, open, stalled on review)
- **#65022** — tracking issue (closed prematurely; closing comment checked API availability but missed the delivery gap)

## What changed

- `api.runtime.acp.spawn(params, ctx)` — starts a new ACP session with thread binding
- `api.runtime.acp.prompt({ sessionKey, text, channel, accountId, threadId })` — sends a follow-up prompt into an existing ACP session via `callGateway({ method: "agent", deliver: true })`
- Both gated behind `plugins.entries.<id>.acp.allowSpawn: true` (default `false`)
- Types, mocks, and tests updated

## Files (7 files, +253 lines)

- `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-acp.runtime.ts` — lazy-loaded ACP runtime helper (new)
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` — wire `acp` namespace into plugin runtime
- `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` — `acp.spawn` and `acp.prompt` types
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts` — gate + delegation tests
- `src/config/types.plugins.ts` — per-plugin `plugins.entries.<id>.acp.allowSpawn` type
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — validation
- `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` — mock alignment

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A trusted plugin can continue an ACP-backed task session through the Gateway agent delivery path so the agent response is delivered into the bound Discord thread, while untrusted plugins cannot call the ACP runtime helpers.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw fork build with the `task-dispatch` plugin enabled and per-plugin config `plugins.entries.task-dispatch.acp.allowSpawn: true`; Discord thread delivery through the Gateway agent pipeline.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Updated the local `openclaw.json` from the old global `plugins.allowAcpSpawn` flag to `plugins.entries.task-dispatch.acp.allowSpawn: true`, then ran `pnpm openclaw config validate`, `cd ~/.openclaw/extensions/task-dispatch && bun run typecheck`, and `bun run build:plugin`. The existing task-dispatch live ACP smoke below exercises the same `api.runtime.acp.prompt({ channel: "discord", threadId })` delivery path this PR exposes.
- **Evidence after fix**: Config/build output and live runtime logs:

```text
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
$ tsc --noEmit
$ bun build src/plugin/index.ts --target=node --format=esm --outfile=build/plugin.mjs --external better-sqlite3
Bundled 4926 modules in 550ms
plugin.mjs  15.81 MB  (entry point)
```

```text
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 initializing ACP session harness=zeus-opencode
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 ACP session initialized in 1766ms
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 binding Discord thread channel=1479519205136138476
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 Discord binding resolved in 1189ms, threadId=1502803134609752165
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 starting ACP turn pollTimeout=120000ms
[POLL] got valid output after 2 polls (18 chars)
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=2f025c40 ACP turn completed in 9209ms, text=18 chars
Result: Discord thread contains agent reply. Task status: done.
Stored output: TD_STRICT_SMOKE_OK
```

Gateway delivery confirmation:

```text
discord: delivered 1 reply to channel:1502803134609752165
message processed: reason=acp_dispatch
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `task-dispatch` still typechecks and builds against the updated per-plugin ACP trust config, OpenClaw accepts the updated config, and the live ACP prompt path delivers the ACP reply into the Discord thread instead of leaving the thread empty.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run a fresh long-lived coding task after changing the trust key from global to per-plugin; the smoke evidence above is the existing task-dispatch live ACP delivery proof for the same runtime prompt path, plus fresh config/build validation for the per-plugin trust change.

### Before (`runTurn()` only — no delivery)

```text
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=89d24685 initializing ACP session harness=zeus-opencode
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=89d24685 ACP session initialized in 2301ms
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=89d24685 binding Discord thread channel=1479519205136138476
[DISPATCH.ACP] task=89d24685 Discord binding resolved in 691ms, threadId=1502783588985344012
[ACP.TURN] starting prompt session=agent:zeus-opencode:acp:e1f7a24f thread=1502783588985344012
[ACP.TURN] ACP completion resolved for run=9c03df2b
[POLL] timed out after 23 polls, returning lastText (0 chars)
Result: Discord thread empty. Task errored.
```

## Security

- `plugins.entries.<id>.acp.allowSpawn` gate (default `false`, checked on every call with the caller plugin id)
- Delegates to the same `callGateway({ method: "agent" })` path native ACP spawn uses
- ACP prompt turns are marked with `acpTurnSource: "manual_spawn"` so explicit plugin-initiated follow-ups use the same ACP turn semantics as native spawn
